title: Pseudo lattice-breathing driven valley switching in 2D ferromagnetic lattices
abstract: Valley manipulation has garnered significant attention due to its exceptionally rich physics and potential device applications.

title: Ultralight and mechanically robust carbon monoliths with aligned microchannels
abstract: Macroporous carbon monoliths with aligned channels are fabricated via ice-templating method. The structure enables mechanically robust, ultralow density, superior dye molecule removal efficiency at high water flux, and efficient heat exchange.

title: Vinylene-linked covalent organic frameworks with fully pyridine-patterned iso-π-conjugation for photoredox catalysis
abstract: Two vinylene-linked covalent organic frameworks, featuring hexagonal and orthorhombic topologies, respectively, exhibit fully pyridine-patterned iso-π-conjugation, enabling the photocatalytic synthesis of benzothiazoles with similar yields.

title: Cu 3 N–Fe nanocube-based electrochemical sensing of cancer metabolites with minute-scale response time
abstract: An Fe-doped Cu 3 N nanocube-based electrochemical platform enabling minute-scale detection of cancer metabolites through direct correlation between catalytic H 2 O 2 sensing and tissue-specific biomarker expression.

title: First observation of dual electrical–optical percolation in metallic nanowire networks
abstract: Beyond electrical percolation, silver nanowire networks exhibit a distinct optical transition at much higher network density. This regime yields a metallic optical response and universal scaling independent of nanowire diameter.

title: Electronic coupling engineering of FeF2@Fe-N-C heterostructure for highly efficient and robust alkaline oxygen reduction
abstract: The development of non-precious metal catalysts with both high activity and exceptional durability for the oxygen reduction reaction (ORR) remains a critical challenge. we propose an electronic coupling engineering strategy...

title: High-performance solution-processed TIIG-based polymer photodetector with detectivity across scalable SWIR applications
abstract: Ultralow-bandgap TIIG-Se-DFT enables RoHS-compliant, solution-processed SWIR photodetectors with high detectivity and minimal fabrication complexity.

title: Lead-free layered halide double perovskites with aromatic organic cations for resistive switching memories and artificial synapses
abstract: Field and light driven migration of halide and Ag + ions in lead free layered double perovskites enables resistive switching, synaptic plasticity and photodetection, allowing a single device to function as memory, synapse and self-powered sensor.

title: Microenvironments between cathode active materials and solid electrolytes for all-solid-state batteries
abstract: Microenvironmental uniformity between cathode active materials and sulfide solid electrolytes determines all-solid-state battery stability and performance.

title: Eutectic electrolyte enables reversible Zn electrodeposition-based electrochromic devices with large optical modulation and robust cycling stability
abstract: The deep eutectic solvent electrolyte enables reversible and stable Zn deposition, leading to an electrochromic device with a large optical modulation of 87% and long-term cycling stability over 2400 cycles.

title: P–N switchable thermoelectric ionogels enabled by microphase separation for intelligent thermal sensing
abstract: In this work, we introduce a microphase-separation strategy that enables in situ p–n switching in ionic thermoelectrics, overcoming their intrinsic unipolar limitation and advancing the design of smart thermal sensors.

title: From material design to smart sensing: recent advances in colorimetric sensors for gas monitoring
abstract: With the continuous advancement of intelligent manufacturing and the growing demands in health monitoring, the need for gas sensors with enhanced portability, wearable integration, and signal visualization has become increasingly prominent.

title: A data-driven approach for the modeling of a ball-milled dispersion of BaTiO 3 nanoparticles
abstract: Targeting the wet-state ball milling of ceramic nanoparticles, a new methodology is proposed connecting data-driven machine-learning prediction to analytical model formulation.

title: Stable high-temperature superlubricity enabled by thermomechanical-induced interfacial graphitization and SiO x -mediated structural locking
abstract: Achieving stable superlubricity of diamond-like carbon (DLC) films in air at elevated temperatures remains a critical challenge due to thermally driven hydrogen loss and oxidation-induced structural degradation.

title: Photoresponsive hydrogels using phototherapy to accelerate wound healing: correlating mechanisms with clinical effects
abstract: Photoresponsive hydrogels (PRHs) enable stage-specific chronic wound healing under light activation by integrating photothermal therapy (PTT), photodynamic therapy (PDT) and photoelectric therapy (PET) via heat, ROS and photoelectrons respectively.

title: Harvesting body heat through clay-based ionic thermoelectric devices
abstract: Here, we demonstrate the possibility of utilizing ionic thermoelectricity for direct conversion of body heat into electricity by using atomically thin 2D channels of reconstructed clay membranes.

title: Hand-gesture recognition using self-powered and single-electrode motion sensors fabricated with InN nanowires
abstract: First demonstration of self-powered and single-electrode piezoelectric motion sensor with InN nanowires and graphene.

title: Transport coupling framework for 3D-printed single-atom catalysts: bridging atomic precision and macroscale functionality
abstract: A transport coupling framework for 3D-printed single-atom catalysts is proposed to bridge atomic precision and macroscale functionality.

title: Sustainable energy harvesting via a scalable Janus photonic metamaterial for thermoelectric generation
abstract: A monolithically integrated Janus photonic metamaterial-based thermoelectric device (JPM-TED) is developed to achieve spectral decoupling, continuous heat transfer and power generation.

title: Predictive Wave Engineering in Polymer Phononic Materials via Viscoelastic-Geometric Coupling †
abstract: Additive manufacturing has emerged as the most accessible option for fabricating polymer-based phononic materials, enabling complex architectures for advanced wave-control applications. However, improper or oversimplified material characterization often limits predictive...

title: Developing real-time IoT-enabled next-generation fire alarm systems using SrBi 4 Ti 4 O 15 /PDMS flexible triboelectric nanogenerators
abstract: A high-performance flexible TENG was designed using a biocompatible pectin polymer and a high-dielectric SBTO–PDMS composite. The device supports the development of IoT-enabled next-generation fire alarm systems for advancing sustainable electronics.

title: Direct polymerization of tyrosine conjugates into melanin-like polymers for efficient protection of photosensitive pesticides
abstract: In this work, a modular and facile method was proposed to prepare a series of melanin-like polymers with excellent ultraviolet (UV) absorption properties through the direct polymerization of tyrosine–oligo(ethylene glycol) (OEG) conjugates.

title: Bio-derived ionic coacervate-engineered cellulose liquid crystal films for electrically reconfigurable microwave absorption
abstract: A bio-derived ionic coacervate-engineered CLCF with an electrically addressable anisotropic architecture achieves integration of renewability, ionic coacervation, and real-time electrical tunability.

title: A bioactive microflower doped triple-network hydrogel for immunomodulation-mediated angio/osteogenesis coordination in mandibular bone regeneration
abstract: This study introduces a Ca/SrP microflower-doped triple-network hydrogel that modulates inflammation, promotes angiogenesis and osteogenesis, and restores mandibular bone defects.

title: Gate-controlled neuromodulatory optical synaptic transistor for adaptive learning and energy-accuracy balance
abstract: This work demonstrates an optical synaptic transistor that mimics biological neuromodulation. Gate-controlled carrier modulation and oxygen-vacancy traps in IGZO enable tunable plasticity, allowing short- and long-term synaptic behaviors.

title: A solar-light driven h-WO 3 /2H-WS 2 -microalgae derived photocatalyst for rapid multi-dye degradation
abstract: Sunlight-driven photodegradation of multiple dyes using microalgae supported h-WO 3 /2H-WS 2 catalysts and PDMS-embedded system, illustrating dye breakdown pathways.

title: Correlated terahertz phonon–ion interactions control ion conduction in a solid electrolyte
abstract: Terahertz rocking modes which enhance ion hopping via correlated ion-phonon interactions are investigated in Li 0.5 La 0.5 TiO 3 using laser-driven electrochemical impedance spectroscopy and laser-driven ultrafast impedance spectroscopy.

title: Quantum dots with photopolymerisable ligands for green-solvent direct photolithography
abstract: Green-solvent processed QDs are anchored with photo-initiating and monomeric dispersing ligands. An i -line triggered radical polymerisation of monomeric ligands enables direct photolithography of chemically robust, high-resolution QD patterns.

title: Temperature-dependent γ′ kinetics evolutions and elemental partitioning in a novel CoNi-based superalloy: An integrated CALPHAD phase-field experimental study
abstract: Co-Ni-based γ/γ′ superalloys are promising candidates for next-generation turbine blades, yet achieving high γ′ stability and low density remains a significant challenge. This study presents a physics-constrained, integrated CALPHAD-Phase-field methodology...

title: Rolling contact bistable passive and active metamaterials
abstract: Novel rolling bistable passive and active elastic strips are integrated in mechanical metamaterials to overcome limitations of conventional negative stiffness mechanisms, delivering large strain capacity, high force density and long fatigue life.

title: Scalable, low-cost ink-based processing of high-performance silver selenide thermoelectrics
abstract: Scalable ink-based processing of Ag 2 Se thermoelectric ink produces dense structures with enhanced transport properties, high power factor, and high power density thermoelectric generator.

title: A bioinspired monolayer gel with efficient omnidirectional moisture-driven actuation for humidity sensing
abstract: A new gel-based highly sensitive moisture-responsive actuator is reported that show unidirectional actuation upon uniform exposure to moisture. The actuator showed hydrogen-bonding induced shrinkage-driven actuation.

title: Supramolecular hydrogen-bonded chiral networks enable blue circularly polarized emission from polymeric carbon quantum dots
abstract: We report a polymeric carbon quantum dots-based CPL-active liquid polarizer. The composites show blue emission (PL QY 64%) and strong CPL ( g lum ≈ ±10 −2 ), and improved resolution (4 lp mm −1 ) with reduced optical crosstalk.

title: Exchange bias induced by the spin glass-like phase in multifunctional ferrimagnetic CoFe 2 O 4 thin films
abstract: A giant intrinsic exchange bias of H EB = 11.1 kOe was reported in ferrimagnetic CoFe 2 O 4 thin films, which results from the unidirectional anisotropy induced by the coupling between the FIM and spin glass-like phases.

title: Circularly polarized-light-driven chiral optoelectronics: encoding, sensing, and neuromorphic processing from an information-flow perspective
abstract: A systematic overview of circularly polarized-light-driven chiral optoelectronics, integrating bioinspired material design with information encoding, sensing, and neuromorphic processing from an information-flow perspective.

title: Machine-learning-guided high-throughput design of asymmetric A–DA′D–A acceptors toward efficient organic solar cells
abstract: A molecule engineering design strategy combining machine learning and high-throughput virtual screening for the A–DA′D–A type NFAs is developed, and new asymmetric acceptors are matched for multiple donors from over one million candidates.

title: A deep learning model for inferring the reverse intersystem crossing rate of TADF organic light-emitting diodes, overcoming the uncertainty of recombination dynamics
abstract: We developed a tandem deep neural network model to infer the reverse intersystem crossing rate coefficient from the transient electroluminescence profile of thermally activated delayed fluorescence organic light-emitting diodes.

title: Facilitating cryogenic blue persistent luminescence in a glassy matrix
abstract: A facile microwave-assisted synthesis approach is employed to incorporate a significant quantity of uniformly distributed shallow traps into an Eu doped MgO–Al 2 O 3 –SiO 2 vitreous matrix, thereby facilitating cryogenic blue persistent luminescence.

title: Orthogonal di-spiro skeleton engineering on suppressing π–π stacking and spectral broadening for high-performance narrowband electroluminescence
abstract: The incorporation of an orthogonal di-spiro π-skeleton in full-color MR-TADF systems effectively suppresses detrimental excimer and aggregate formation, enabling OLEDs with high efficiency and stable narrowband emission spectra.

title: High-entropy alloys in a radial mesostructured TiO 2 support for efficient hydrogen evolution
abstract: A spatial confinement strategy encapsulates PtCuFeCoNi high-entropy alloy nanoparticles in ordered mesoporous TiO 2 channels. The catalyst delivers excellent hydrogen evolution activity and durability in alkaline electrolyte.

title: Ion-modulated oxide-based neuromorphic transistors for spatiotemporal information processing
abstract: Unlike energy-intensive von Neumann systems, the human brain efficiently processes complex spatiotemporal information utilizing slow, dissipative ionic dynamics. To emulate this, ion-modulated oxide-based neuromorphic transistors have emerged as a compelling...

title: Near-infrared high-resolution imaging via deep learning based on a broadband achromatic metalens
abstract: This work presents a framework integrating a large-aperture polarization-independent achromatic metalens and a super-resolution network to achieve high-resolution imaging, promising for virtual/augmented reality and integrated photonics.

title: Time–temperature–transformation-layering diagrams: a design tool for lightweight multi-layered, single-material polymeric structures
abstract: TTTL diagrams add a transport band to TTT diagrams to design layered structures. An example employing polymer crystallization and foaming agent transport during foaming is proposed to obtain a layered crystalline-amorphous-foamed part.

title: A microfluidics-enabled photonic crystal biosensor for simplified, point-of-care diagnosis of a blood biomarker
abstract: POCT is hindered by sample preparation and complex workflows. Here, we integrate on-chip filtration and photonic crystal fluorescence for automated AFP detection from one drop of blood (LOD 0.1 ng mL −1 , R 2 = 0.981 vs. ELISA).

title: Nitrogen vacancies in graphitic carbon nitride and their role in heterogeneous photocatalysis
abstract: Nitrogen vacancies critically affect photocatalysis in g-C 3 N 4 . Large calculations show long-range structural relaxation is key to reproduce optical signatures and photoluminescence quenching explaining enhanced photoreduction and suppressed oxidation.

title: Solar thermal conversion gels for atmospheric water harvesting: fundamental principles and applications
abstract: This review presents recent advances in solar thermal conversion gels for atmospheric water harvesting, including design principles and compositions, mechanisms of adsorption/desorption, optimization strategies and diverse application scenarios.

title: Sustainable advances in nanostructure-doped polymer hydrogels for fog harvesting: materials innovation, mechanistic insights and emerging applications
abstract: The progress and challenges of nanostructure-doped polymer hydrogels have been reviewed for fog harvesting, emphasizing their mechanical and functional improvements compared with traditional hydrogels for sustainable environment applications.

title: Carbon nanotube-enabled coatings for advanced anti-icing and deicing applications
abstract: Predictive design principles based on Hansen solubility parameters for carbon nanotube-based coatings enable scalable, durable, and energy-efficient anti-/de-icing solutions, enhancing safety and climate resilience across critical infrastructures.

title: Non-radiative recombination energy losses in Y-series asymmetric acceptor-based organic solar cells
abstract: The molecular engineering of Y6 has marked a paradigm shift in the development of non-fullerene acceptors for organic solar cells, enabling remarkable enhancements in efficiencies through its distinct architecture and adjusted voltage loss.

title: Large-scale experimental validation of thermochemical water-splitting oxides discovered by defect graph neural networks
abstract: To identify promising metal oxides for thermochemical water-splitting, we propose a workflow involving defect machine learning model training, high-throughput computational screening, material down-selection, and experimental validation.

title: Weavable ion–electron hybrid gel fibers with adjustable thermopower and high output voltage for wearable energy harvesting
abstract: An ionic hydrogel fiber with adjustable thermopower, high conductivity and good mechanical properties for a 4.9 V body-heat-harvesting textile.

title: Metamaterials with in situ tunable bending properties
abstract: We report a method for designing meta-beams/plates that offer global tunable bending stiffness. This principle enables tunable static bending deformation and vibrational modal shapes and frequencies for beam and plate structures.

title: Engineering multifunctional nanoporous polymer networks through covalent nanoparticle linking for ultrafast iodine capture
abstract: This work introduces a new synthetic concept that simultaneously enhances both surface area and functional group accessibility in porous polymer networks, leading to improved performance in applications such as rapid iodine capture.

title: Low-activation-energy bipolar organic nanostructures for high-capacity and ultralong-life aqueous calcium-ion batteries
abstract: Low-activation-energy bipolar organic nanostructures delivered consecutive electron delocalization pathways to fully expose built-in multi-redox sites, affording high-capacity and ultralong-life aqueous Ca-ion batteries.

title: Synergistic LSPR and interfacial built-in electric field in an Au/WO 3 film for a high-performance flexible electrochromic smart window
abstract: Au nanoparticles/WO 3 heterofilms combine localized surface plasmon resonance and built-in electric field enable ultrafast, efficient and flexible electrochromic smart windows.

title: Friction-assisted electrochemical oxidation of iridium surfaces for enhanced catalysis
abstract: Friction-assisted anodic oxidation enables site-selective formation of amorphous IrO x on Ir surfaces. The resulting IrO x exhibits enhanced acidic OER activity, offering a simple route for in situ fabrication of active oxide coatings.

title: Unraveling atomic-scale origins of interfacial properties in CsPbBr 3 /M 2 O 5 (M = Nb, Ta) heterojunctions: a combined first-principles and experimental approach
abstract: First-principles calculations, in conjunction with experimental investigations, were utilized to probe the structural, stability, electronic, and optical properties of CsPbBr 3 /M 2 O 5 heterostructures.

title: Significantly improved energy storage performance of polyetherimide based composite materials by introducing Na 0.5 Bi 0.5 TiO 3 filler surface-modified with organic molecular semiconductors
abstract: In this work, PCBM was used as a modifier to surface modify NBT for the first time, and then NBT@PCBM was introduced into the PEI matrix. This method provides more possibilities for the development of high-quality dielectric materials.

title: Frontal curing of aluminosilicate–epoxy composites for extreme underwater environments
abstract: A new class of aluminosilicate composites was developed for underwater on-demand frontal curing, showing great potential for rapid underwater repair.

title: Reprogrammable shape memory ion gels via physical entanglement of ultrahigh molecular weight polymers
abstract: Shape memory ion gels comprising ultrahigh molecular weight polymers and ionic liquids are fabricated via a facile one-pot polymerisation, delivering outstanding shape-memory functions, including triple shape-memory behaviour and shape reprogramming.

title: High latent heat and recyclable shape stable phase change materials with dynamic ester bonds for thermal management
abstract: A novel, recyclable shape-stabilized PCM synthesized via a carboxyl/epoxy reaction exhibits high latent heat (121.0 J g −1 ) and, with 30 wt% BN, enhanced thermal conductivity (1.41 W m −1 K −1 ) for efficient thermal management.

title: Strong and flexible graphene oxide paper for humidity responsive origami metamaterials
abstract: We develop strong and flexible graphene oxide (GO) papers for decimeter-scale and humidity-responsive origami metamaterials. Topological design enables multiple kinematic modes and state indication/memory functions in GO metamaterials.

title: Controlling the intersystem crossing/reverse intersystem crossing (ISC/RISC) competition to achieve efficient red metal-free phosphorescent molecules
abstract: Systematic modulation of ISC/RISC in D–A–D molecules with O, S, and Se enabled a tunable emission from fluorescence to TADF and then to RTP.

title: Ferroelectric nanofibers with nanoconfined tellurium nanobeams for mechanical and thermal energy harvesting and wearable healthcare
abstract: Ferroelectric nanofibers with confined tellurium nanobeams exhibit enhanced dipole alignment with anisotropic charge transport, enabling efficient dual-mode mechanical and thermal energy harvesting for wearable power devices.

title: Solvent-triggered off-to-on circular polarization activation via scattering modulation in polymer-based chiral photonic particles for encryption and authentication
abstract: Solvent-triggered, scattering-mediated switching of circularly polarized light in chiral photonic crystals for dynamic optical encryption and authentication.

title: Liquid crystal elastomer-based reversible metamorphosis for loss-less droplet manipulation
abstract: We developed liquid crystal elastomer-based programmable superomniphobic surfaces that enable loss-less and reversible manipulation of aqueous and organic droplets.

title: Stochastic dynamics of nanoparticle catalysis: a discrete-state perspective
abstract: Intrinsic structural heterogeneity, surface fluxionality, and multi-site cooperativity influence nanocatalytic behavior and its catalytic activity. Stochastic modeling can efficiently describe how catalytic performance emerges from these phenomena.

title: Fluorine-doped 3D honeycomb carbon network for long-duration aqueous zinc–iodine batteries
abstract: F-doped porous carbon network (F–Cnet) shows enhanced cycling stability, improved reaction kinetics, and excellent long-duration energy storage (LDES) performance for aqueous Zn–I 2 batteries.

title: Towards nickel–titanium shape memory alloys for coatingless icephobic materials
abstract: This study introduces a nickel–titanium shape memory alloy as a durable, coatingless material with low ice adhesion for icephobic applications.

title: Rapid ambient direct growth of HKUST-1 via atmospheric pressure plasma treatment
abstract: Atmospheric pressure plasma enables rapid, electrolyte-free growth of HKUST-1 films on copper substrates. The plasma–liquid–solid interface synchronizes anodic metal dissolution and ligand deprotonation for efficient interfacial coordination.

title: Physically reconfigurable synaptic plasticity and learning in stretchable neuromorphic systems
abstract: A physically reconfigurable neuromorphic platform enables the programming of synaptic plasticity via gate electrode assembly. This strategy provides task-adaptable functions for versatile neuromorphic computing.

title: PAN/MXene: a potent piezo-gen and cogent piezoelectric separator for self-chargeable supercapacitors
abstract: We have successfully synthesized a flexible multifunctional PAN/MXene nanofiber-based piezo-supercapacitor utilizing electrospinning. Surface ion dynamics drive potential differences, enabling efficient self-charging.

title: Defect-engineered ZnO/BTO photoanodes for enhanced solar-driven photoelectrochemical desalination of high-salinity coal chemical wastewater
abstract: This work discussed the application of ZnO/BTO heterojunction photoanodes with dual vacancies (Biv and Ov) in solar-driven photoelectrochemical desalination of high-salinity wastewater from coal chemical industry.

title: Piezoelectric [trimethylchloromethyl ammonium][CdCl 3 ] microrods/PVC composites for high-fidelity ultrasound detection in the mid-frequency range
abstract: Piezoelectric TMCM–CdCl 3 microrods/PVC composite films were fabricated, demonstrating a high ultrasound detection frequency-domain fidelity of 74.1%, surpassing conventional materials.

title: A low-RCS coding metasurface utilizing 3-D printed ABS shells and carbonyl iron powder/polyimide composite patches via hybrid mechanisms
abstract: Based on ABS and CIP/PI composites, the proposed coding metasurface leverages a hybrid mechanism—integrating energy absorption with scattering control—to achieve broadband RCS reduction.

title: Nanoconfinement as an electrolyte-state selector in hard carbon for sodium storage
abstract: Nanoconfinement in hard carbon selects local electrolyte coordination and thereby controls interphase survival during sodium storage.

title: Suppressing thermal transport in nonporous polymer hybrids by limiting thermally accessible vibrational modes
abstract: Low thermal conductivity in nonporous polymer hybrids is enabled by limiting thermally accessible vibrational modes, without relying on porosity or individual interfacial thermal resistance.

title: Electrostatic complexation of conjugated polyelectrolytes
abstract: A review of electrostatic complexation of conjugated polyelectrolytes is presented with an emphasis on their physical behavior and potential applications.

title: A general structural decoupling strategy toward ultra-long blue circularly polarized room-temperature phosphorescence
abstract: The “structural decoupling” strategy—using a non-conjugated methylene spacer to decouple chiral units from the π-backbone—enables efficient blue circularly polarized room-temperature phosphorescence with a high dissymmetry factor.

title: Multidimensional, multilevel information storage and encryption in auxetic liquid crystal elastomers
abstract: This study presents a novel approach for multidimensional, multilevel information storage and encryption using auxetic liquid crystal elastomers. The information is encoded by UV-masks and decoded through distinct strain magnitudes and directions.

title: Methane functionalization in heterogeneous photocatalysis
abstract: Methane functionalization is the “holy grail” of chemistry. This review highlights recent progress in heterogeneous photocatalytic methane conversion, focusing on photocatalyst design, structure–activity relationships, and reaction mechanisms.

title: The piezoionic diode: field-driven amplification of mechano–ionic conversion
abstract: A piezoionic effect diode is a new class of heterostructure mechanoelectrical conversion devices. By leveraging a built-in electric field to actively drive directional ion transport, the device achieves an amplified self-powered piezoionic output.

title: Non-integer-dimensional architected materials enabling synergistic acoustic, mechanical, and fluid coupling
abstract: A non-integer dimensional design paradigm enables intrinsic multifunctionality by embedding acoustic, mechanical, and fluid coupling directly into geometry, offering a scalable and fabrication-friendly route for architected metamaterials.

title: Construction of cross-scale hierarchical ordered biomimetic architectures enabled by a fluid shear force field-assisted sacrificial template method
abstract: A fluid shear force field-assisted sacrificial template method enables the construction of cross-scale hierarchical ordered biomimetic architectures, addressing the challenge of the multi-scale 3D ordering alignment in 3D-printed materials.

title: Operando synchrotron radiation insights into multiscale regulation of CO 2 electroreduction
abstract: This review establishes an integrated research paradigm for CO 2 RR, coupling multiscale regulation strategies with operando synchrotron radiation characterization to guide rational electrocatalyst design.

title: Defect-induced subgap state engineering in neuromorphic metal-oxide phototransistors for in-sensor color processing
abstract: A full-color neuromorphic image processor was realized using a homostructure metal oxide phototransistor via subgap engineering with interstitial alkali metal elements.

title: Chiral-polarized photonic chips via organic crystal heterojunctions
abstract: Organic single-crystal heterostructures integrate gain and waveguiding, achieving circularly polarized lasing and efficient chiral signal transport for photonic devices.

title: Harnessing weak monomer miscibility to create porous microdomains in polymer electrolytes for zinc-ion batteries
abstract: In situ phase separation and water-induced self-assembly create a gradient-channel amphiphilic ionogel that regulates Zn 2+ flux and interfacial chemistry for ultra-stable zinc batteries.

title: Structurally engineered yolk–shell mesoporous silica rods with liquid and nanoparticle cargo
abstract: This study presents design strategies for hollow mesoporous and yolk–shell silica rods. These structures can encapsulate both liquid cargos and diverse nanoparticles, enabling tailored functionalities according to the incorporated species.

title: Programmable ion–protein networks from sodium caseinate: a sustainable platform for soft functional materials
abstract: Ion–protein coordination transforms sodium caseinate into programmable hydrogels with ion-dependent nanoscale packing, tunable mechanics, and soft-sensing performance, establishing a sustainable platform for functional soft materials.

title: A panther chameleon-inspired Vis-NIR light modulation hydrogel with enhanced photothermal effects and pH/temperature monitoring for bacterial infection theranostics
abstract: A panther chameleon-inspired Vis-NIR light modulation hydrogel is developed for enhanced photothermal therapy with real-time colorimetric pH/temperature monitoring for bacterial infection theranostics.

title: Visual luminescence thermometry enabled by phase-transition-activated cross relaxation of Tb 3+ ions
abstract: This work demonstrates that a monoclinic-to-tetragonal phase transition in LiYO 2 :Tb 3+ activates cross-relaxation of the 5 D 3 state, inducing a blue-to-green emission change and enabling ON–OFF thermal switching and filter-free thermal imaging.

title: Band-gap engineering of Bi-MOFs via anthraquinone integration for boosting photocatalytic H 2 O 2 production over a donor–acceptor–acceptor junction
abstract: An S-scheme heterojunction was constructed by integrating RF resin with anthraquinone-coordinated Bi-MOF. This design enabled directed electron flow and efficient charge separation, boosting photocatalytic H 2 O 2 production to 1523 µmol g −1 h −1 .

title: π-Conjugation-extended benzoimidazoisoindolone-based n-type ladder polymers for highly air-robust aqueous sodium-ion electrochromic energy storage
abstract: A novel BAL-EC demonstrated high-performance electrochromic energy storage. Sequential electron transfer involving preferential carbonyl reduction, followed by imidazole ring reduction, enables synergistic regulation of electrochromic energy storage.

title: Multicolor room temperature phosphorescence in dibenzothiophene derivative-doped elastic binary polymers for multi-step encryption displays
abstract: A series of elastomers exhibiting long-lived, multicolor (blue to red) room-temperature phosphorescence was fabricated via the rational integration of two complementary polymers.

title: Two-dimensional semiconductor photodetectors: from physical mechanisms to intelligent sensing system integration
abstract: We summarize advances in 2D heterojunction photodetectors, from mechanisms to modulation and integrated applications. 2D semiconductors enable efficient carrier modulation and show promise in imaging, sensing and neuromorphic optoelectronics.

title: Zinc indium sulfide (ZnIn 2 S 4 ): a promising candidate material towards next-gen photodetection
abstract: A systematic review on photodetectors based on ZnIn 2 S 4 has been conducted, providing promising building blocks for implementing next-gen optoelectronic systems.

title: A moisture stable high-entropy halide electrolyte with performance recovery capability for all-solid-state batteries
abstract: The practical application of halide solid electrolytes (HSEs) is hindered by their poor moisture stability.

title: Reconfiguration of d-orbital states drives non-radiative energy dissipation in semiconductors
abstract: Engineering d-orbital defect states through multi-transition-metal doping can efficiently regulate the dissipation of energy from photoexcited charge carriers through non-radiative pathways.

title: Metal–organic frameworks and derivatives as next-generation materials for electrochemical energy storage
abstract: The global pursuit of carbon neutrality demands transformative clean energy solutions, with advanced energy storage materials at the forefront.

title: Piezophototronic modulation in a 2D Ruddlesden–Popper perovskite: a pathway to strain–light synergistic self-powered intelligent sensing
abstract: Interfacial coupling between low-dimensional halide perovskites and ferroelectric matrices provides a powerful route to amplify electromechanical and optoelectronic responses for self-powered systems.

title: Effects of Sn 4+ solid solubility mechanisms on the electromechanical and energy storage performance of (Ba 0.85 Ca 0.15 )(Ti 0.92 Zr 0.08 )O 3
abstract: Solid solubility mechanisms and interlinked charge-compensation processes and defect equilibria of Sn 4+ doped (Ba 0.85 Ca 0.15 )([Ti 0.92− x Sn x ]Zr 0.08 )O 3 ( x = 0.00–0.10) influence the piezoelectric properties and energy storage performance.

title: Binary Cu/boron enhanced graphene quantum dots as a biomimetic nanocatalyst for Trp catabolism to indole-3-aldehyde: mechanism and specificity
abstract: Multienzyme-like nanocatalysts offer a useful platform for studying complex biochemical transformations. Herein, we demonstrate that a Cu/boron-GQD nanozyme can effectively mimic the native catabolism of tryptophan to indole-3-aldehyde.

title: Thermoresponsive hydrogel-powered zero-energy greenhouses: intelligent shading and water irrigation
abstract: By synchronizing atmospheric water capture, on-demand irrigation, and adaptive radiative shading within a single material, thermoresponsive hydrogels enable the development of sustainable strategies for next-generation greenhouses.

title: Atomically dispersed Ru on defective CdS for photocatalytic solar fuel production coupled with hydrazine degradation
abstract: A CdS-Ru catalyst with dual-functional sites enables solar fuel production and hydrazine oxidation.

title: Thermal rectification of shape memory polymer composites by programmable conductivity modulation
abstract: Shape memory polymer composites achieve large thermal conductivity variation and high thermal rectification efficiency via temperature-driven volumetric filler fraction changes.

title: Eigenmode steering in spatiotemporal gain–loss acoustic metamaterials
abstract: A spatiotemporal gain–loss framework for eigenmode steering in coupled acoustic resonators is introduced. Depending on the nonlinear gain strength, the system operates in directed energy routing into target modes or Rabi-like oscillations.

title: Advancing electrospun nanofiber scaffolds for next-generation tissue engineering: from trend analysis to multifunctionalization and hybrid fabrication
abstract: Tissue engineering is an emerging and integrated field for the repair of defective tissues, which benefits from the interdisciplinary development of biomaterial and engineering techniques.

title: Light-permissive piezoelectrics: advances in Pb-based transparent ceramics and crystals for next-generation devices
abstract: This review highlights advances in lead-based transparent ceramics and single crystals, detailing synthesis strategies, optical–electromechanical coupling, and their emerging roles in next-generation optoelectronic, sensing, and energy devices.

title: Intracellular gelation of biomaterials: strategies, characterization, and biomedical applications
abstract: Driven by progress in gelation strategies and mechanistic comprehension, controlled intracellular gelation enables versatile engineering of functional cell-based biomaterials.

title: LiFSA–KFSA binary molten salt enables durable lithium–antimony batteries at 80–100 °C
abstract: A mid-temperature Li‖Sb molten-salt battery delivers high energy and power with long-term stability, enabled by reversible Sb phase evolution and a stabilizing inorganic SEI.

title: Design and engineering of biomimetic aloe vera sponges via recombination of functionalized peel and gel for enhanced wound healing
abstract: Here, we report a supramolecular, plant-inspired strategy to build Aloe vera-mimicking sponges that couple nanoscale therapeutic functions with a macroporous scaffold for stepwise wound healing.

title: Fullerene derivative integration controls morphological behaviour and recombination losses in non-fullerene acceptor-based organic solar cells
abstract: Fullerene derivatives PC 61 BM and PCBC6 in PM6 L8-BO ternary-based organic solar cells regulate active layer morphology, suppress trap assisted and non-radiative recombination, and increase performance and operational/thermal stability.

title: Structurally tailored nanocomposite sorbent enabling high-energy-density thermochemical storage in e-thermal banks for electric vehicle applications
abstract: Hierarchically structured salt-in-matrix sorbents enable fast kinetics, leak-proof, ultra-high-energy-density microwave-powered thermochemical storage for onboard heating and cooling in electric vehicles.

title: Robust superamphiphobic coatings in confined and chemically inert tubular geometries enabled by a dynamic circulation coating strategy
abstract: Dynamic circulation coating enables robust superamphiphobic coatings on the inner walls of narrow, chemically inert tubes via flow-controlled interfacial assembly.

title: Advanced polymeric membranes for CO 2 separation: fundamentals, materials, and practical challenges
abstract: Bridging material design with thin-film engineering, this review highlights strategies to overcome stability and scalability barriers in polymeric membranes for CO 2 separation, accelerating their deployment for industrial decarbonization.

title: Monomer conversion enables chemical recycling of high-performance plastics and carbon fiber composites without monomer separation
abstract: High-performance engineering plastics exhibit outstanding mechanical robustness and chemical stability, yet their robust structures often hinder chemical recyclability. Here we report a monomer conversion strategy that enables chemical recycling of...

title: Interfacial charge phonon coupling in Bi 2 Se 3 /WSe 2 nanohybrids for bifunctional near-infrared devices
abstract: Photodetection and photomechanical actuation are essential for next-generation optoelectronics and soft robotics, yet their integration within a single device remains challenging.

title: Printable, self-healing and recyclable PEDOT:PSS/polyurethane composites for durable bioelectronics
abstract: Printable PEDOT:PSS/polyurethane conductors combine room-temperature self-healing, high stretchability, and recyclability, enabling skin-conformal electronic tattoos and reusable freestanding bioelectrodes.

title: Adaptive memory of hydrogels with tunable hysteresis
abstract: A one-pot synthesis method was proposed to develop adaptive memory by preparing dual-responsive hydrogels. The thermal hysteresis window can be adjusted from approximately 0 °C to 17.6 °C by changing the pH stimulus.

title: Materials derived from a sulfur vulcanization of biochar
abstract: The sulfur-volcanized biochar (BS materials) is prepared using a cheap, sustainable and scalable method of synthesis in batches on a few grams scale. The mechanical properties surpass that of other structural materials such as plastics and steel.

title: Approaching the Sabatier optimum via a triple-defect synergistic strategy for enhanced oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: A triple-defect CoMOF (Fe/Al doping, Co vacancy) optimizes oxygen evolution, achieving ultralow overpotential. Theory confirms its cooperative defects idealize adsorbate energies, offering a universal design paradigm.

title: Charge state-dependent ion condensation near conjugated polymer backbones
abstract: By combining operando resonant X-ray scattering and molecular dynamics (MD) simulations we show that counterion positions within the lattice of a prototypical organic mixed ionic/electronic conductor (OMIEC) change as a function of its charge state.

title: AI-assisted design of 3D NPR lattice materials with programmable mechanical properties using irregular unit cells
abstract: AI-assisted framework to inversely design 3D NPR lattice materials composed of irregular cells with programmable negative Poisson's ratio and energy absorption, validated across scales via additive manufacturing and in situ testing.

title: Leveraging solid–liquid interaction to fabricate drug-microsphere in site encapsulated bone-repair scaffolds
abstract: We fabricate drug-microsphere in site encapsulated bone-repair scaffolds (CHP@Drug), in which a solid–liquid interaction triggered by vortex oscillation realizes in site preparation and encapsulation of drug-loaded microspheres.

title: 3D manipulation of cell spheroids using laser-actuated microrobots
abstract: We present laser-actuated microrobots embedded in cell spheroids that induce three distinct thermophoretic motion modes, enabling selective and biocompatible spheroid assembly.

title: Dual-layer self-healing composites with temperature-responsive intelligent broadband microwave absorption
abstract: A smartly tunable bilayer composite achieves interlayer-complementary microwave dissipation for ultra-broadband absorption.

title: All-optically modulated PDVT-10/IGZO heterojunction synapses for neuromorphic applications
abstract: All-optically modulated PDVT-10/IGZO heterojunction synapses with excellent conductance modulation ratio and stability for neuromorphic recognition and preprocessing applications.

title: Unveiling the synergistic piezo-phototronic dynamics in indium selenide incorporated poly(vinylidene fluoride) nanocomposites for next-generation energy harvesting
abstract: Piezo-phototronics leverages the synergy of piezoelectricity and photoexcitation to enable advanced optoelectronic functionalities in flexible materials and devices.

title: Supersensitive natural rubber-based piezoresistive pressure sensor with hierarchical sandwich structure
abstract: The burgeoning fields of wearable devices, health monitoring and human-motion detection have spurredinterest in flexible piezoresistive sensors, which in turn have raised concerns about the sustainable development of flexible electronics.

title: Intrinsic mechanics of 3D-printed porous polymer-derived ceramic metamaterials
abstract: An experimental–numerical strategy extracts the intrinsic bulk Young's modulus of porous 3D-printed polymer-derived ceramics independent of their internal porosity, attributing to prediction of mechanical performance of metamaterials.

title: Confined polymerization: multidimensional regulation, advanced measurements and cutting-edge applications
abstract: Confined polymerization, as an innovative polymerization strategy, achieves precise control over the reaction pathway and microscopic structure of the product by confining the polymerization reaction within the physical space of a micro–nano scale.

title: Zwitterionic polyurethane thin films enabling wet conformal adhesion to dynamic tissues
abstract: Zwitterionic polyurethane thin films with a thickness of 200–1200 nm are developed that combine high elasticity, antifouling capability, and wet conformal adhesion on dynamically deformable tissues in vivo .

title: Economic evaluation and catalyst design for hybrid water electrolysis systems
abstract: Hybrid water electrolysis boosts efficiency by replacing OER with anodic oxidation reactions (AORs). This review categorizes AORs and discusses catalysts, design, and techno-economics.

title: Ultrahigh-sensitivity organic photodetectors processed with a non-halogenated solvent for a self-powered mechano-optoelectronic coupled sensing system
abstract: An o -xylene-processed ultra-sensitive organic photodetector is integrated with a mechanoluminescent unit to form a fully self-powered sensing system that enables accurate motion-state perception and gesture recognition.

title: A multifunctional self-healing binder with strong interfacial interactions and structural stability for high-energy-density silicon anodes
abstract: A multifunctional self-healing binder enables enhanced structural integrity and stable cycling in high-energy-density silicon anodes.

title: Engineering oxygen vacancies in Au/MnO 2 catalysts for complete formaldehyde removal at near-freezing temperatures
abstract: Complete HCHO removal at 0 °C under flow conditions using Au/MnO 2 nanowires.

title: Electro-driven fibrous soft actuators towards flapping-wing control for insect-scale robots
abstract: Electro-driven fibrous soft actuators based on liquid crystal elastomer with excellent comprehensive properties are developed, showing great potential for application in flapping-wing control of insect-scale robots.

title: Uncovering a third function of the transistor beyond switching and amplification: coupled photo-responsive oscillators for in-sensor computing
abstract: Coupled twin photo-responsive oscillators are introduced as a fundamental component for a coupled oscillator network with in-sensor computing.

title: Variable vortex optical metasurface: synergistic control of topological charge and focal position
abstract: Metasurface with dynamically reconfigurable vortex light field.

title: Bioinspired crystallization refinement strategy enables ion-conductive elastomers with an ultra-wide strain-insensitive window for reliable signal transmission and adaptive sensing
abstract: A bioinspired crystallization-mediated regulation framework enables intrinsically strain-insensitive ion-conductive elastomers with ultra-wide windows via a rigid-soft transport network, allowing reliable signal transmission and adaptive sensing.

title: Interfacial microenvironment and catalyst modulation for efficient hydrogen peroxide synthesis via mimicking oxidase catalysis
abstract: We report here an air–liquid–solid triphase system for efficient H 2 O 2 synthesis via mimicking HCOOH-oxidase catalysis. Simulations and experiments reveal a higher interfacial O 2 concentration of the triphase system, leading to a higher performance.

title: Photopolymerized helical acrylate networks enable stable and full-color circularly polarized luminescence in perovskite nanocrystals
abstract: A photopolymerized helical acrylate framework simultaneously stabilizes perovskite nanocrystals and induces color-tunable CPL. This confinement strategy offers a scalable platform for chiral optoelectronics and quantum photonics.

title: High-flux nickel foam electrodes with asymmetric binder engineering for efficient electrolyte transport and CO 2 regeneration in reactive carbon capture
abstract: Reactive carbon capture (RCC) from bicarbonate solutions offers a highly efficient pathway for carbon-neutral fuel production by bypassing energy-intensive CO 2 desorption steps. However, conventional carbon paper (CP)-based electrodes suffer from...

title: Epoxy polymers as versatile hydrophobic components for tough phase-separated hydrogels
abstract: Phase-separated hydrogels with epoxy polymer as the hydrophobic component achieve excellent mechanical properties and multiple functions.

title: Correction: Moderately polarized carborane-MOF with inverse C 2 selectivity for one-step polymer-grade ethylene purification
abstract: Correction for ‘Moderately polarized carborane-MOF with inverse C 2 selectivity for one-step polymer-grade ethylene purification’ by Changhong Liu et al. , Mater. Horiz. , 2026, 13 , 473–479, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5MH01641J.

title: Interface-modified NiMgO x layers with dibenzocarbazole molecules for high-efficiency perovskite light-emitting diodes
abstract: A dibenzocarbazole-based 4PADCB is introduced at the NiMgO x /perovskite interface to enhance hole injection and suppress interfacial quenching. This interfacial strategy improves the peak EQE of green PeLEDs from 5.5% to 14.4%.

title: Interface engineering via pre-engineered black phosphorus quantum dots for highly efficient carbon-based hole-transport-layer-free perovskite solar cells
abstract: Interface engineering via pre-engineered black phosphorus quantum dots leads to efficiency improvement from 13.8% to 17.1% for planar HTL-free C-PSCs.

title: Planar rotor-enabled quenching-resistant NIR-II fluorophores for high-contrast bioimaging and efficient cancer phototheranostics
abstract: A novel small-molecule fluorophore, BTP-2TCF, featuring planar tricyanofuran rotors, exhibits quenching-resistant NIR-II fluorescence and ROS generation. Its nanoparticles demonstrate superior in vivo phototheranostic efficacy.

title: Synergistic enhancement of high-temperature stability and energy-storage performance in polypropylene dielectric films via molecular trap engineering
abstract: Uniformly dispersed electron-deficient AOHBP molecules stabilize polypropylene through chain pinning and deep charge trapping, enabling excellent dielectric energy-storage performance under high electric fields and elevated temperatures.

title: From real-time monitoring to precision therapy: the application of piezoelectric systems in cardiovascular diseases
abstract: This review systematically integrates the materials, mechanisms and applications of piezoelectric systems in cardiovascular diseases and further distills design principles for clinically translatable devices.

title: Cobalt oxide–supported iridium oxide nanoparticles with strong metal oxide–support interaction for efficient acidic oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: The strong metal oxide–support interaction (SMOSI) induced at the interface can accelerate deprotonation for high oxygen evolution reaction (OER) activity.

title: Integrated conductive hydrogel soft actuators for remote photothermal actuation and multimodal self-sensing
abstract: Integrating rapid actuation, mechanical robustness, and multimodal sensing within a single hydrogel system—a capability essential for next-generation adaptive machines.

title: Enabling gradient biomineralization and prestress with a macroscopic matrix: the fish swim bladder for bioprocessing-inspired fabrication
abstract: Fish swim bladder, a macroscopically structured natural matrix, enabled gradient hydroxyapatite mineralization and intrinsic prestress, facilitating bioprocessing-inspired fabrication of bulk composites with enhanced mechanical performance.

title: Mechanically robust and high latent heat solid–solid phase change materials via a H-bonding collaborative strategy for energy storage and conversion
abstract: Solid–solid phase change materials (SSPCMs) historically struggle to achieve an optimal balance between mechanical robustness and efficient thermal energy storage (TES) capacity.

title: A multifunctional organic additive with diversified elements (F, S, P, and N) for high-performance PEO/LiTFSI electrolytes
abstract: A multifunctional additive (perfluorophenyl)trithiophosphate (FPS), which reduces PEO crystallinity, broadens the electrochemical window, and constructs a F/S-rich SEI, offers a rational design strategy for high-performance solid electrolytes.

title: Architecting ordered mesoporous materials via additive manufacturing
abstract: This work reports a scalable additive manufacturing platform that can architect ordered mesoporous materials with diverse chemistries from commodity precursors.

title: Polyurethane elastomers based on triple reversible networks with accelerated self-healing by photothermal conversion
abstract: Thermoplastic polyurethane (TPU) supramolecular networks with hybrid hard segments composed of 1,4-benzoquinone dioxime (BQDO) and/or ureidopyrimidinone (UPy) have been successfully achieved through pre-polymerization and subsequent chain extension.

title: Additive manufacturing of polymers and composites for applications in aerospace and aeronautics
abstract: This comprehensive review emphasizes the significant role of additive manufacturing (AM) in transforming the production methods of aerospace and aeronautical real components.

title: Structural polymorphism in bent-core liquid crystals: from helical nanofilaments to heliconical-layered microcylinders with light-responsive properties
abstract: Azobenzene-containing bent-core liquid crystals exhibit chain length-dependent structural polymorphism, showing a curvature-driven transition from helical nanofilaments to heliconical-layered microcylinders.

title: Achieving strength and toughness limits of anisotropic microstructured alumina ceramics through interface engineering
abstract: Nacre-like all-ceramic metamaterials can be programmed by two interface descriptors: the stiffness ratio and the toughness/strength ratio between matrix and platelet to control their toughness.

title: Self-assembled monolayers for bio-analytical SERS applications: from single to mixed component SAMs
abstract: Mixed self-assembled monolayers (SAMs) is a powerful strategy for controlling the surface chemistry of plasmonic nanomaterials. This review presents the recent advances in mixed SAMs for surface-enhanced Raman spectroscopy.

title: Dimensionality-Driven Decoupling of Electronic-Ionic Transport in a Cs 4 PbBr 6 /CsPbBr 3 Heterojunction
abstract: Harnessing the interplay between electronic and ionic transport in lead halide perovskites is pivotal for advancing next-generation iontronics. Here, we demonstrate a pronounced disparity in charge transport dynamics enabled by...

title: Revolutionizing carbon nitride-based photocatalysts: design strategies for energy conversion and environmental applications
abstract: This review highlights descriptor-driven defect engineering in carbon nitride-based photocatalysts. It analyzes how defects regulate electronic structures to optimize their activities for sustainable applications.

title: Tuning surface reactivity pathways through molecular inhibitor redosing for precision nanopatterning
abstract: Comparison of area selective atomic layer deposition (AS-ALD) selectivity between conventional inhibitor single dose and re-dosing strategies.

title: Supramolecular crafting of peptides as novel antimicrobial materials
abstract: This review summarizes the recent progress of supramolecular peptide assemblies, focusing on the molecular design, self-assembly behavior and applications of antibacterial peptide nanostructures.

title: Synergistic substrate design for enhanced mechanical and optical stability in island-bridge stretchable displays
abstract: A multi-covalently crosslinked polyurethane (MCPU) integrating reversible hydrogen bonding and covalent anchoring suppresses interfacial delamination and substrate sagging in an island–bridge stretchable display.

title: A thermally engineered NbO x memristor with CdTe 2 interlayers for high-accuracy ECG arrhythmia detection
abstract: This work demonstrates that thermal engineering of NbO x memristors via CdTe 2 interlayers enhances threshold stability by over 30.9% and enables a 90.0%-accuracy ECG arrhythmia detection system, advancing physically grounded neuromorphic hardware.

title: In situ generated bubble-mediated porous ionically conductive hydrogels for hydrogel-based electronics
abstract: A porous hydrogel with oriented porous channels was constructed, demonstrating high sensitivity in pressure sensors and stable charge–discharge performance in zinc-ion hybrid supercapacitors.

title: Nanostructured cellulose ionogels with selective anion confinement for high-efficiency thermoelectric harvesting
abstract: Nanostructured cellulose ionogels were developed via thiol–ene click of allyl cellulose and POSS-8SH. This design enables selective anion confinement and microphase separation, decoupling ion/heat transport for high thermoelectric performance.

title: Colloidal quantum dots and their assemblies for circularly polarized luminescence
abstract: This review summarizes recent advances in design strategies and structure–property relationships of CPL-active quantum dots, highlighting the importance of NIR-II emission and AI-assisted design for next-generation CPL materials.

title: Correction: Shape programming of liquid crystal elastomers by two-stage wavelength-selective photopolymerization
abstract: Correction for ‘Shape programming of liquid crystal elastomers by two-stage wavelength-selective photopolymerization’ by Tom Bruining et al. , Mater. Horiz. , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5MH01907A.

title: Geometry-invariant optical properties of dielectric meta-atoms with antireflection coatings
abstract: A geometry-invariant regime in dielectric meta-atoms with antireflection coatings is demonstrated. Phase, transmission, and reflection depend only on effective diameter, remaining independent of cross-sectional shape across different geometries.

title: Ag 2 Se/conjugated polyelectrolyte heterojunction films for high-performance flexible thermoelectrics
abstract: Interfacial ion-electron coupling in a planar Ag 2 Se/conjugated polyelectrolyte heterojunction creates an electron-rich transport channel, enabling record flexible thermoelectric performance.

title: Versatile liquid metal composites constructed using an organic–inorganic hybridization strategy for flexible electronics
abstract: Liquid metals (LMs) with high electrical conductivity and fluidity are regarded as promising materials for developing multifunctional materials.

title: Fluids at electrode/membrane interfaces suppress alcohol crossover in CO electroreduction
abstract: We introduce pure-water interfaces in zero-gap electrolyzers to suppress alcohol crossover. By leveraging differences in crossover driving forces, we achieve >90% pure alcohols and acetate for 6 h at 200 mA cm −2 without losing Faradaic efficiency.

title: A 3D printed Cu evaporator support for record-high interfacial solar evaporation
abstract: A 3D-printed evaporator capable of harvesting thermal energy from the bulk water, far exceeding the solar energy input, is fabricated to deliver high evaporation rates even without solar illumination, enabling all-weather clean water production.

title: Shaping topological structures and intrinsic properties of aramid nanofiber films through fluid flow templating
abstract: A fluid flow templating strategy is employed to shape the structural units and corresponding topological architectures of ANF films during reprotonation, synchronously improving their intrinsic thermal and mechanical properties.

title: Advances in Co-based electrocatalysts for sustainable ammonia synthesis: mechanisms, design strategies, and emerging applications
abstract: This review covers recent progress in various nanostructural Co-based catalysts for nitrate electroreduction to ammonia, addressing reaction mechanisms, catalyst design, and emerging applications.

title: Competition between mirror symmetry breaking and translation symmetry breaking in ferroelectric liquid crystals with increasing lateral substitution
abstract: Increasing the length of lateral alkoxy chains in ferroelectric liquid crystals reveal a competition between the formation of layered and heliconical structures.

title: A simple copolymer with integrated high impact-stiffening, broadband damping, and active perception for advanced protection
abstract: Impact-stiffening materials with broadband damping are essential for next-generation intelligent protective systems.

title: Programming magnetic composites and phase change materials for multimodal haptic interfaces with integrated self-sensing toward adaptive and proprioceptive haptic feedback
abstract: Enabled by programmable soft magnetic composites, the compact, skin-conformal, self-sensing haptic interface seamlessly delivers three distinct modes of haptic feedback: normal, rotational shear, and skin stretch.

title: High-sensitivity, dual-mode, flexible, electrooxidized cellulose-based electronic skins with compatible mechanical properties and antimicrobial activity for wide-temperature applications
abstract: Flexible wide-temperature sustainable ionogel with compatible mechanical properties, high sensitivity, antimicrobial activity and intensified transfer.

title: Size-dependent pyrolysis pathways of Co-triazolate MOFs tailor carbon-matrix morphology and catalytic site distribution
abstract: Tuning a Co-triazolate MOF precursor from ∼50 nm to 2 µm steers pyrolysis toward 1D fibers or 3D frameworks, creating distinct Co–N–C sites and HER activity—highlighting precursor morphology as a key lever in catalyst design.

title: Decoupling thermoelectric parameters in novel ionic layered materials: a charged monolayer stabilization strategy for enhanced anisotropy
abstract: We propose a cation-stabilized monolayer network principle to design anisotropic ionic thermoelectrics. Screening 370 candidates revealed 26 stable materials, like p-type β-Mg 3 Sb2, with enhanced directional transport.

title: Ion-electron coupling in a MXene/silk nanofluidic hydrovoltaic device for enhanced electricity generation
abstract: A MXene/silk nanofluidic hydrovoltaic device leverages ion-electron Coulomb drag at the solid–liquid interface to boost output voltage to 593 mV with dilute ionic droplets and further to 622 mV under infrared irradiation.

title: Interface-governed electromechanical coupling in bioinspired hierarchical piezoelectric poly( l -lactide) architectures
abstract: Interface-engineered soft piezoelectric architectures leverage chemically activated interfaces to amplify ultrasound-driven electromechanical coupling, enabling bioinspired, scalable communication with cells efficient.

title: Bio-inspired 3D-printed TPU/LiCl hierarchical porous cones for high-performance atmospheric water harvesting
abstract: Illustration of the fabrication process and working principle of the bio-inspired hierarchical porous cone.

title: Site-selective thionation and doping enabled tunable full-color emission in nonconventional luminophores
abstract: Tunable full-color emission of nonconventional luminophores is achieved via site-selective thionation and controlled doping, showing promise for anti-counterfeiting, information encryption, and high-resolution latent fingerprint visualization.

title: Self-healing and sense-healing: metal–organic framework-filled polymers for robust and durable soft robotics
abstract: Soft robots demand damage-resistant materials. This review introduces “sense-healing”, the autonomous recovery of sensory function alongside structural repair, enabled by MOF-polymer composites, offering a roadmap for durable soft materials.

title: Reentrant phase transition via light–heat antagonism in a single polymer system
abstract: Light-heat antagonism in spiropyran isomerization enables reentrant phase transitions in a polymer system, showing LCST at low and UCST at high temperatures. This allows programmable volumetric transitions and oscillatory deformation in hydrogels.

title: Monolithic metasurface for ultra-stable nuclear spin frequency for miniaturized NMR optical pumping
abstract: A monolithic phase-polarization multiplexed metasurface enables simultaneous beam collimation and high-efficiency circular polarization generation for miniaturized NMR optical pumping.

title: Spectroscopy-guided optimization of copper-based catalysts for low-temperature CO 2 recycling to CO
abstract: The reverse water–gas shift (RWGS) reaction provides a sustainable route for CO 2 valorization by producing CO, a key intermediate for various industrial applications.

title: The strong polarization effect of lanthanide metals for efficient alkaline hydrogen evolution
abstract: The strong polarization effect of lanthanide metals for efficient alkaline hydrogen evolution by polarizing H 2 O and repairing hydrogen-bond networks was demonstrated.

title: Novel ambipolar polymers for detection beyond 1000 nm with organic phototransistors
abstract: Fine-tuning the TQ-based polymer structure yields balanced charge transport, enabling simple integrated circuits. Due to their extended NIR-II absorption, high-performing NIR organic phototransistors were developed.

title: Photoplasticity behavior in inorganic semiconductors: unraveling fundamental mechanisms across ionic and covalent systems
abstract: Illumination drives carrier migration toward dislocation cores, altering core energetics and mobility, which produces opposite photoplasticity behaviors, such as hardening in ionic semiconductors and softening in covalent semiconductors.

title: An optically controlled synaptic device based on a PdSe 2 /α-In 2 Se 3 vdW heterostructure FET
abstract: Visual synaptic devices show great promise for advanced neuromorphic hardware, offering a viable solution to the von Neumann bottleneck.

title: 2D chiral organic salts with a large piezoelectric response
abstract: The simple chiral organic salt ( R / S )-4-bromo-α-methylbenzylammonium chloride exhibits a large piezoelectric coefficient d 33 of 49 pC N −1 , surpassing most molecular piezoelectric materials.

title: Nanofiller induced molecular lubrication strategy for ultrathin capacitor films with high energy density and minimal thickness
abstract: Significant progress has been made in dielectric polymer film capacitors, primarily focusing on reducing film thickness and enhancing energy density.

title: Overcoming the energy–durability trade-off in aluminum foil anodes via hierarchical laminated grain boundaries
abstract: A hierarchical laminated grain-boundary network in Al–1 wt% Si foils synchronizes lithium-ion transport and mechanical response, overcoming the long-standing energy–durability trade-off in aluminum-foil anodes.

title: Structural dimensionality governing CO 2 /C 2 H 2 separation in MWW zeolites
abstract: Engineering the zeolite dimensionality reveals that 2D MWW layers outperform their 3D analogues by enhancing CO 2 /C 2 H 2 separation efficiency.

title: Layered hybrid lattice architectures for broadband electromagnetic absorption and improved structural stiffness
abstract: A hybrid layered lattice design achieves integrated electromagnetic absorption and mechanical stiffness by tuning geometric layer arrangement, enabling multifunctional and lightweight metamaterial absorber applications.

title: A magneto-responsive deformable coating with lubricant self-replenishment towards on-demand scale repellence
abstract: The magneto-responsive deformable coating with lubricant self-replenishment property achieves long-term anti-scaling performance in complex fluid environments, delivering a scale removal efficiency of approximately 96.8%.

title: Clean production of chlorine (Cl 2 ) and hypochlorous acid (HOCl) from photocatalytic and photoelectrochemical seawater splitting
abstract: Production of Cl 2 and HOCl from photocatalytic and photoelectrochemical seawater splitting.

title: Persistent radicals in organic photonic synaptic transistors for pattern recognition of electrical and multicolor optical stimuli
abstract: An organic photonic synaptic transistor based on photoactive persistent radical accessing multiple electronic/spin states which is capable of dendritic parallel learning under different patterns of the optical and electrical incoming stimuli.

title: Electrochemical recovery and regeneration of polyethylene terephthalate materials
abstract: Electrocatalytic reforming efficiently converts waste PET plastics into high-value chemicals such as formic acid and glycolic acid, creating a 'waste-resource' circular economy path with economic and environmental benefits.

title: Synergistic interphase regulation with a KSeCN bifunctional additive for stable and high-performance lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: Potassium selenocyanate (KSeCN) synergistically regulates electrode/electrolyte interfaces, facilitating the conversion kinetics of lithium polysulfides and suppressing the shuttle effect, enhancing the cycling stability of Li metal.

title: Radiation-hard organic electronics with fullerene-based semiconductors
abstract: We demonstrate an exceptional radiation hardness of fullerene semiconductors in lateral resistor and field-effect transistor device geometries under ultra-high doses of 60 Co g-rays, unlocking their potential for extreme-environment electronics.

title: Electrically responsive multilayer soft actuators using a solvent-free high dielectric permittivity polysiloxane ink
abstract: Multilayer soft dielectric elastomer actuators (DEAs) consist of stacked elastic capacitors that convert electrical energy into mechanical work.

title: High-efficiency nondoped near-ultraviolet organic light-emitting diodes based on spiro luminogens with high-lying reverse intersystem crossing
abstract: An effective molecular design strategy based on a spiro skeleton for exploring robust organic near-ultraviolet (NUV) luminogens is proposed, and high-efficiency nondoped organic light-emitting diodes are achieved based on the new NUV luminogens.

title: Enhanced toughness in highly entangled hydrogels via non-covalent molecular hooks
abstract: Molecular hooks in the shape of PEG side chains are introduced into highly entangled UHMW poly(acrylamide) networks, producing shear-resistant, tough, yet stretchable hydrogels through a combination of hydrogen bonding and topological entanglements.

title: Harnessing diffraction with metamaterial noise barriers for enhanced sound attenuation
abstract: We introduce a metabarrier that exploits, rather than suppresses, diffraction. A vertical resonator array converts diffracted sound into surface-guided waves, achieving broadband noise attenuation and redefining diffraction as a functional mechanism.

title: An engineered membrane separation system using a binder-free immobilized heterojunction photocatalyst for enhanced energy-efficient organic dye separation
abstract: A binder-free strategy fabricates functional copper foam integrating heterojunction photocatalysts with excellent interfacial stability, enabling continuous, rapid treatment of massive organic dyes via membrane-like columnar technology.

title: High-dimensional multiplexed metamaterial for cross-media all-sound communication
abstract: A high-dimensional multiplexed metamaterial is proposed for modulating all dimensions of sound waves traversing water–air media, enabling high-speed, real-time cross-water–air communications with sound as the sole information carrier.

title: Harnessing precision in hydrogel architectures through reversible-deactivation radical polymerisation techniques
abstract: Hydrogels, with unique 3D network structures and excellent compatibility with diverse biological environments, have attracted widespread interest for applications in biomedicine, drug delivery, soft robotics, tissue engineering, and bioelectronics.

title: Synergistic enhancement of the mechanical properties of polyurethane ionogel by halometallate ionic liquid-induced phase separation and hydrogen bond cross-linking
abstract: Ionogels, an emerging and rapidly developing type of soft material, are widely employed in flexible electronic devices.

title: Catalytic carboxylation of sp 2 and sp 3 C–H bonds with CO 2 under atmospheric pressure conditions: an integrated experimental and theoretical study
abstract: Rational design of a 2D-COF composed of active Ag( i )-based cyclic trinuclear clusters as SBUs and basic N sites in the framework, enabling CO 2 fixation through C(sp 2 )–H/C(sp 3 )–H carboxylation, to valued carboxylic acids, is demonstrated.

title: A non-percolating interfacial microcapacitor architecture decouples polarization enhancement from leakage suppression in humidity-tolerant polymer films
abstract: Interface-confined carbon nanotubes (CNTs) form non-percolating microcapacitors that enhance charge storage while suppressing humidity-induced leakage in melt-processable triboelectric polymer films.

title: Expedited Zn 2+ diffusion in electrostatically shielded tunnels triples the capacity of spinel ZnMn 2 O 4
abstract: Strong electrostatic repulsion limits Zn 2+ diffusion in spinel cathodes. We build Mn-vacancy tunnels with charge-depletion zones that screen this repulsion, enabling fast ion transport while maintaining structural stability.

title: Oriented membranes with in-plane aligned nanosheets for high-energy-efficiency zinc-based flow batteries
abstract: [100]-Oriented mixed-matrix membranes (MMMs) with in-plane aligned ZIF nanosheets are fabricated via a shear-flow-induced orientation method, which overcomes the conductivity-selectivity trade-off and enables record-breaking battery performance.

title: In situ nanoscale heterostructure engineering for additive manufacturing of dynamic adaptive alloys
abstract: A novel in situ nanoscale heterostructure engineering (INSHE) strategy utilizing laser-excited reactions to achieve enhanced printability, strength, ductility, and functional performance without post-processing.

title: Suppression of interfacial layers in ZrO 2 /TiN capacitors by atomic layer deposition using ligand-engineered Zr precursors for scalable DRAM
abstract: As dynamic random-access memory continues to scale down, the physical thickness of capacitor dielectrics decreases, and controlling the low- k interfacial layer at the ZrO 2 /TiN interface becomes increasingly critical.

title: Unveiling the sensing mechanism at the molecular level: a DFT study on the disaggregation of perylene diimide radical anion pimers
abstract: Density functional theory calculations reveal that low-barrier hydrogen bonds drive the disaggregation of perylene diimide radical anion pimers, offering a novel design strategy for π-radical-based organic sensors.

title: Bi-synergistic ligand-mediated passivation of surface defects for highly efficient and stable cesium-lead-iodide perovskite quantum dot photovoltaics
abstract: A universal ligand simultaneously passivates both perovskite quantum dot and ZnO interfaces, enabling highly efficient and exceptionally stable photovoltaics.

title: Bioadhesive polydopamine-vitamin A derivative hydrogels reprogram the wound microenvironment for scarless wound healing and hair follicle regeneration
abstract: Melanin-like polydopamine nanoparticles deliver vitamin A derivatives within an imine-crosslinked hydrogel dressing, dampening inflammation and ROS to enable scarless healing and de novo hair follicle regeneration.

title: Recyclable near-body temperature eutectic system with double positive temperature coefficient effect for personalised thermal regulation
abstract: A recyclable nanocomposite exhibits a dual positive temperature coefficient effect near body temperature. Bio-based eutectic transitions enable tuneable thermal window for smart sensing, heating/cooling and multifunctional protection.

title: In-operando dipole orientation for bipolar injection from air-stable electrodes into organic semiconductors
abstract: Doping a dipolar molecule into an organic semiconductor enables charge transfer from high-work-function, air-stable electrodes. During operation, dipole rearrangement facilitates efficient bipolar charge injection without interlayers.

title: Flexoelectric polarization–electric field coupling-driven phase transformation in epitaxial films
abstract: Reversible control of phase transformation driven by flexoelectric polarization–electric field coupling in complex oxide heterostructures.

title: A Threefold Approach to Boost Photo-harvesting Efficiency in Covalent Organic Frameworks via Strategic N-Centre Regulation
abstract: The strategic development of metal-free photocatalysts with efficient charge-carrier separation and transfer is crucial for high-performance solar-energy conversion. In this regard, covalent-organic frameworks (COFs) have emerged as promising candidates; however,...

title: Beyond the melting point annealing of poly(vinylidene fluoride) for enhanced piezoelectric performance
abstract: The use of high temperature, beyond the melting point, and high stress on PVDF films to improve the piezoelectric performance. New insight into how macroscopic and microstructural effects PVDF piezoelectricity.

title: Lorentz-tail engineering toward over 10-year data retention with minimum loss in ferroelectric HZO
abstract: We demonstrate over 93% retention for over 10 years in ferroelectric HZO without extrapolation. By precisely controlling the Lorentz-tail, we propose a device-operational approach to improve long-term reliability in ferroelectric memory applications.

title: A polarized broken framework for electrical energy harvesting from mechanical friction
abstract: A polarized covalent organic framework with defects having abundant electronegative atoms served as a negative layer in a triboelectric device coupled with Nylon-11 to effectively convert body movements into electrical energy.

title: Self-electrophoresis-propelled and self-built electric field-enhanced photocatalytic nanomotors for round-the-clock environmental remediation
abstract: The self-built electric field of Janus nanomotors drives self-electrophoretic motion and enhances light-activated generation of reactive oxygen species, enabling round-the-clock water decontamination of estradiol and E. coli .

title: Scalable ambient-dried aramid aerogel fibers with hierarchical networks for ultrahigh toughness and thermal insulation
abstract: Proton-donor-assisted solvent exchange enables ambient-dried aramid aerogel fibers with hierarchical porous networks, achieving ultrahigh toughness and stretchability while maintaining excellent thermal insulation.

title: Inverse vulcanization enabled self-motivated polysulfide silane: an ultra-efficient interfacial architecture for silica-filled elastomer hybrids
abstract: A self-motivated polysulfide silane that can readily migrate onto a silica surface via hydrogen bonds for promoting silanization is synthesized through inverse vulcanization, leading to an ultra-low hysteresis loss in the nanocomposites.

title: Photon-controlled memristive synapses: recent progress toward brain-inspired neuromorphic computing
