abstract: This review highlights recent advances in optoelectronic memristive synapses, detailing the underlying mechanisms, materials, device architectures, performance metrics, and applications, along with challenges and future trends.

title: LadderGen: a large-scale generative library of ladder polymers for membrane separations
abstract: LadderGen expands ladder polymer chemical space to ∼0.8m candidates via reaction templates and generative models. ML screening and MD validation identify candidates exceeding the Robeson upper bound, enabling efficient membrane discovery.

title: Moderately polarized carborane-MOF with inverse C 2 selectivity for one-step polymer-grade ethylene purification
abstract: A novel moderately polarized carborane MOF (CB-Zn-DPG) was successfully prepared for one-step C 2 H 4 (>99.99%) purification from a ternary C 2 H 2 /C 2 H 4 /C 2 H 6 mixture.

title: Operando neutron radiography validates a parameter-free transport–kinetics model for thick solid-state battery cathodes
abstract: Tortuosity-weighted interfacial flux for lithium predicts through-thickness Li gradients in thick all-solid-state cathodes without fitted parameters, reproducing neutron radiography and guiding designs to suppress transport-limited reaction fronts.

title: Defect-rich Co–MoS 2− x O x @NC fiber heterostructures derived from in situ grown metal-organic frameworks towards high electromagnetic wave absorption
abstract: Defect-rich heterointerfaces integrated with adjustable crystalline phases and atom vacancies provide a promising strategy to boost electromagnetic wave (EMW) absorption.

title: Controlling the oxidation and chemistry of photodeposited CuO X species via charge density modulation
authors: 'Szymon Dudziak', 'Marta Kowalkińska', 'José D. Gouveia', 'Adam Ostrowski', 'Miguel Gomez-Mendoza', 'Marcin Łapiński', 'Aleksandra Szkudlarek', 'José R. B. Gomes', 'Víctor A. de la Peña O'Shea', 'Anna Zielińska-Jurek'
abstract: Aliovalent doping of the TiO 2 host allow to fine-tune chemical interactions between the photodeposited CuO X and water/oxygen molecules. The state-of-the-art efficiencies are reported for the degradation of numerous organic pollutants.

title: Size-dependent electrobending in piezoceramics mediated by gradient defect dipoles
abstract: A mechanical model accurately describes the size dependence of electrobending, shifting its understanding from a phenomenological observation toward a quantitative, mechanics-based framework.

title: Na 4 Fe 3 (PO 4 ) 2 (P 2 O 7 ) cathode for sodium-ion batteries: from crystal structure to high-energy-density design and solid-state battery application prospects
abstract: This paper provides a comprehensive analysis of NFPP, covering its crystal structure, sodium storage mechanisms, and challenges and explores its application potential within solid-state batteries, offering strategic insights for future advancement.

title: High-quality acoustic energy harvesting via topology-optimized quasi-bound states in the continuum
abstract: Quasi-BIC confinement engineered via topology optimization suppresses radiation loss, producing ultrahigh- Q acoustic resonance and markedly improved piezoelectric energy harvesting performance.

title: Memristive behavior of a V 2 C MXene/PANI:PSS composite and its applications in ammonia detection
abstract: Memristive devices that integrate gas sensing with neuromorphic functionality have been identified as a promising route to compact, low-power, and multifunctional artificial olfaction.

title: Performance optimization of bijels as a novel type of catalyst support structure
abstract: Based on lattice Boltzmann simulations, we demonstrate that porous catalyst supports acquired from spinodally-derived architectures outperform their stochastic counterparts.

title: Evolving electrocatalytic nitrate-to-ammonia conversion on Cu- and Co-based catalyst engineering with paired electrolysis approaches
abstract: This review outlines a roadmap for efficient electrocatalytic nitrate reduction to ammonia, analyzing Cu- and Co-based catalyst design, reaction mechanisms and paired electrolysis systems for transformative energy efficiency and process economics.

title: Isomeric organic–inorganic indium bromide single crystals with delayed and dual colour emission
abstract: Isomeric indium bromide single crystals with fast green and delayed yellow emissions are developed. Face-shared InBr 4 tetrahedra in the green-emitting crystal and unshared InBr 4 tetrahedra in the yellow-emitting crystal distinguish the isomers.

title: A high-entropy gradient filler metal enables high-strength joints of Ti 2 AlNb and GH4169 alloys
abstract: Vacuum brazing with an innovative filler alloy is critical for manufacturing high-precision aerospace components requiring exceptional comprehensive performance, yet achieving superior strength in Ti 2 AlNb/GH4169 brazed joints remains challenging.

title: Multistable mechanical metamaterials for sound absorption
abstract: Multistable tunable acoustic metamaterial for low-frequency broadband sound absorption.

title: Highly sensitive X-ray detectors with polymer-perovskite-embedded flexible teflon membranes
abstract: We report a dual-action mechanism using PMA in polymer-perovskite composites within PTFE membranes. This yields flexible X-ray detectors combining high sensitivity with excellent mechanical durability and operational stability.

title: Interface Engineering in Organic Electrochemical Transistors toward Multifunctional Bioelectronics
abstract: Bioelectronics bridges electronic systems and living organisms by enabling bidirectional transduction between ionic/biochemical signals and electronic outputs. Among emerging platforms, organic electrochemical transistors (OECTs) have become particularly promising for biointerfaces...

title: Designing aperiodic metamaterials using mechanical neural networks
abstract: Mechanic neural network design aperiodic metamaterials by first learning the desired behaviors via tuning the stiffness of their active beams. Passive beam design with those same stiffness are accordingly organized within the final design.

title: Additive manufacturing of MXene electrodes: from rheology-tunable nanoinks to size-scalable integrated electronics
abstract: Additive manufacturing (AM) has emerged as a transformative strategy for translating nanoscale materials into wafer-scale functional architectures, empowering the scalable fabrication of advanced electronics and energy systems.

title: Highly conductive and flexible Ag NWs/PBFDO:PEOx composite transparent electrodes enabling efficient interfacial tunneling injection for OLEDs
abstract: Highly conductive and flexible Ag NWs/PBFDO:PEOx composite transparent electrodes enabling efficient interfacial tunneling injection for OLED.

title: Efficient afterglow across liquid, hydrogel, and solid states enabled by naphthalimide-doped polyacrylate copolymer emulsions under UV/visible excitation
abstract: Naphthalimide-doped polyacrylate copolymer emulsions were developed to achieve efficient afterglow across liquid, stretchable hydrogel, and solid states under both UV and visible excitations.

title: Strong hybridization driving unusual enhanced negative thermal expansion in PbTiO 3 -based ferroelectrics
abstract: The distinct covalent characteristics of Fe–O and Co–O bonds give rise to divergent temperature-dependent unit cell volume responses: 0.5PbTiO 3 –0.5BiFeO 3 exhibits strong nonlinear NTE, while 0.6PbTiO 3 –0.4BiCoO 3 displays colossal volume contraction.

title: Surface-based stress tomography of architected metamaterials via physics-constrained generative learning
abstract: Physics-constrained generative learning reconstructs internal 3D stress fields from surface images, enabling non-destructive stress tomography in architected metamaterials.

title: 3D printed photo-sensitized microfiltration membranes for simultaneous water filtration and pathogen management
abstract: 3D printing nanoporous thin film monoliths allows for direct incorporation of stimuli responsive moieties to develop photo-sensitized catalytic membrane reactors.

title: Organic cathodes for rechargeable magnesium metal batteries: materials, mechanisms, and prospects
abstract: This review highlights organic cathodes for magnesium batteries, focusing on CO/CN redox chemistry and its role in regulating ion transport and electrochemical performance.

title: Molecular-switch-embedded organic Schottky barrier transistors for a high switching ratio
abstract: Molecular switches (DAEs) are integrated into vertical organic Schottky barrier transistors (OSBTs) with a transparent DMD electrode, enabling record-high photoprogrammable switching ratio (>6.4 × 10 4 ) and excellent fatigue resistance over 100 cycles.

title: Scalable nanoporous superhydrophobic films toward extreme icing conditions at −141°C and icing wind tunnel
abstract: Superhydrophobic surfaces offer a promising passive anti-icing alternative, yet they frequently fail to sustain the nonwetting Cassie‑Baxter state under dynamic icing conditions. Conventional approaches for robust superhydrophobic anti-icing coating rely...

title: Orientation-engineered epitaxial BiVO 4 thin films for efficient photoelectrochemical glycerol valorization
abstract: Orientation-engineered BiVO 4 photoanodes establish crystallographic anisotropy as a governing factor for charge separation and glycerol oxidation performance.

title: Strain stiffening and compression-induced softening of composite fibrous hydrogels derived from rod-shaped nanoparticles and a synthetic copolymer
abstract: Strain stiffening and compression-induced softening of the fibrous hydrogels were controlled by varying the degree of intrafibrillar crosslinking.

title: Green surface-engineered bio-acrylate architectures for high-efficiency triboelectric energy harvesting
abstract: Tailored plant-derived polymers with engineered surface structures enable degradable triboelectric nanogenerators that deliver up to 250 V and 65 mW m −2 , demonstrating a scalable strategy for sustainable energy-harvesting materials.

title: Generative inverse design for microstructure control in precursors for Li- and Mn-rich layered-oxide cathodes
abstract: A closed-loop image-guided framework integrates generation, quantification, and optimization to enable predictive synthesis and inverse design of Li-ion cathode precursors.

title: Injectable hyaluronic acid-based hydrogels with carbon dots and an iron complex for embolization
abstract: Although transarterial chemoembolization is a prevalent treatment for hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC), its efficacy is limited by inadequate vascular infiltration, inconsistent embolization, and lack of therapeutic synergy.

title: Core–shell Mg–Ni carbonate supercapacitor materials by concurrent Ni recovery and CO 2 mineralization
abstract: Coupling CO 2 mineralization with nickel recovery yields a MgCO 3 @NiCO 3 core–shell carbonate that can be directly valorized as a supercapacitor electrode, turning a separation challenge into a sustainable materials opportunity.

title: Strategic B-site cation engineering in Sillén–Aurivillius perovskite oxyhalides for ultra-high efficiency piezocatalytic H 2 O 2 production
abstract: Strategic B-site cation engineering in Sillén–Aurivillius Bi 5 Ti 2 O 11 Cl control over electronic structure and polarization, yielding a record piezocatalytic H 2 O 2 production rate (15 041 µmol g −1 h −1 ) and creating a new ferroelectric design paradigm.

title: Recent advances in the thermal management performance of polymer-based composite materials
abstract: Summary of polymer-based thermal management materials.

title: From the up-converting multimodal luminescent thermometer to ratiometric visual power density meter based on Er 3+ ,Yb 3+ emission
abstract: In this study, it is demonstrated how the thermally induced changes in the spectroscopic properties of Na 3 Sc 2 (PO 4 ) 3 :Er 3+ ,Yb 3+ can be used for construction of the luminescence based visual optical density meter.

title: Coordinated sub-cycle modulation atomic layer deposition of atomically homogeneous GeTe 9 thin films for high-performance OTSs
abstract: Atomically homogeneous amorphous GeTe thin films are realized via coordinated sub-cycle modulation ALD, synchronizing deposition with in situ diffusion. This enables high-performance OTSs and offers new possibilities for 3D integration.

title: Hydrogel-integrated multimodal physiological and modulation systems
abstract: Hydrogel-integrated bioelectronics provide stable and adaptive interfaces for multimodal physiological sensing and neuromodulation.

title: Correction: Decoupling thermoelectric parameters in novel ionic layered materials: a charged monolayer stabilization strategy for enhanced anisotropy
abstract: Correction for ‘Decoupling thermoelectric parameters in novel ionic layered materials: a charged monolayer stabilization strategy for enhanced anisotropy’ by Yaobo Li et al. , Mater. Horiz. , 2026, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5MH02247A.

title: Origin of inverse emission behaviour in strain-engineered zero-dimensional tin halides: all-inorganic vs. hybrid
abstract: Inverse luminescence in 0D n s 2 tin halides arises from the ground-state structural distortion and lone pair exposure, which enhances STE emission in all-inorganic systems, whereas excited-state-driven STE destabilization suppresses emission.

title: Geometry-engineered hourglass-shaped cracks enable wide-linearity wearable strain sensors
abstract: In this study, by operating the ohmic contact modulation of predefined hourglass-shaped cracks in serpentine curves, we present a novel resistive strain sensor that exhibits high sensitivity, excellent linearity, and ultralow detection limit.

title: Microphase water control utilizing highly hydrophilic anion-exchange ionomers
abstract: A hydrophilic 3D ionomer achieves high AEMWE performance and stability under dry cathode feed, outperforming commercial ionomers and offering a new approach to water and mass transport management.

title: A bird nest-inspired and fully biomass coating with an interpenetrating fibrous network for thermal insulation and universal fire retardancy
abstract: Bioinspired by birds’ nests, a fully biomass-based nanostructured coating is engineered for universal flame retardancy, adhesive properties and thermal insulation, without using organic solvents and traditional flame-retardant elements.

title: Mechanistic origin of charge separation and enhanced photocatalytic activity in D–π–A-functionalized UiO-66-NH 2 MOFs
abstract: Combining photo-EPR, transient photoluminescence, and theory reveals how D–π–A functionalization of linkers reshapes excited-state pathways in MOFs, where triplet charge-transfer states govern long-lived carriers that enable photocatalytic activity.

title: Construction of hierarchical 2D/2D few-layered MoS 2 /S-doped g-C 3 N 4 heterojunctions for enhanced photocatalytic formaldehyde removal
abstract: Modification of graphitic carbon nitride (g-C 3 N 4 ) with semiconductor nanomaterials has been widely investigated as an effective method to enhance its photocatalytic activity.

title: Sustainable and scalable polyphenolic bioadhesives for hemostasis and surface functionalization
abstract: Coupling-agent-free covalent assembly enables direct aqueous formation of polyphenol–amine materials. This process proceeds within minutes and supports liter- scale production, delivering substrate-agnostic coatings and strong wet bioadhesion.

title: Biomimetic low-porosity framework membranes for efficient power generation and acid recycling from industrial wastewater
abstract: A bioinspired low-porosity phosphated COF membrane enables fast proton transport while blocking heavy metal ions, achieving efficient acid recovery and high-performance osmotic energy conversion for wastewater treatment.

title: Is the use of sacrificial agents a sustainable practice in scalable photocatalytic hydrogen production from water?
abstract: This article exposes the thermodynamic, economic, and environmental unsustainability of sacrificial agent-dependent hydrogen production, and advocates for priority allocation of resources for hydrogen production from overall water-splitting.

title: Silicon-stabilized three-dimensional covalent networks in high entropy diborides
abstract: Metal diborides typically contain transition metals in between the graphene-like boron sheets. High entropy enables the incorporation of the main group metalloid silicon and overcomes chemical coordination preferences.

title: A bioinspired dual-modal laser-induced graphene tactile sensor for high-precision multimodal object recognition
abstract: Inspired by trichoid sensilla on insect antennae, a bioinspired trichoid tactile sensor (BTTS) that enables simultaneous acquisition of triboelectric and piezoresistive signals for material and shape recognition was developed.

title: C14-Laves phase high entropy alloys for hydrogen storage: a review
abstract: This review summarizes the current research status of C14-HEAs for hydrogen storage, focusing on microstructural design strategies, manufacturing processes, hydrogen storage properties and their potential challenges and applications.

title: Short-wave infrared organic photodetectors based on n-type small molecular semiconductors
abstract: N -type molecular semiconductors offer tunable properties, high purity and mobility for low-cost SWIR-OPDs as inorganic alternatives. This review summarizes their progress in molecular design, device engineering, applications, and future perspectives.

title: Dynamic surface reconstruction governs the hydrogen evolution activity of Mo 2 C electrocatalysts in alkaline media
abstract: The ability of Mo 2 C to stabilize and regenerate defective MoO 2 surface domains without succumbing to excessive oxidation defines its alkaline HER activity.

title: Locking of poly (sodium acrylate) hydrogels for underwater ultrastretchability but overwater non-flexibility
abstract: Although water is an essential component of hydrogels, developing hydrogels showing distinct deformability depending on the surroundings remains interesting and challenge. Herein, a structural remolding strategy of hydrophilic poly (sodium...

title: Advancements of nanoparticle-based adhesive materials
abstract: This review highlights the recent advances in nanoparticle (NP)-based adhesive systems, analyzing the distinct impact of NPs on adhesive materials from a NP-centric perspective and discussing the opportunities and challenges for future development.

title: Machine learning to design metal–organic frameworks: progress and challenges from a data efficiency perspective
abstract: This review critically examines work at the intersection of machine learning (ML) and metal–organic frameworks (MOFs).

title: Extreme-mixing-boosted CMAS corrosion resistance and thermophysical properties in high-entropy rare-earth disilicates
abstract: High-entropy rare-earth disilicates with extreme element mixing are synthesized by a laser-driven technique. The remarkable CMAS corrosion resistance and thermophysical properties makes them promising for thermal/environmental barrier applications.

title: Hybrid metamaterials for decoupled electromagnetic-acoustic wave manipulation: achieving four negative constitutive parameters
abstract: We propose and experimentally realize a modular, replaceable design paradigm for hybrid metamaterials that enables independent control of electromagnetic and acoustic properties within a unified metamaterial unit cell.

title: Compositional and structural control toward boosting inner-grain prestress and releasing the inter-lattice strain of an FGH99 diffusion-bonded superalloy
abstract: Inner-grain pre-stress was induced by a customized lattice distortion-enhanced strain-hardening effect. Structural coherence with interfacial coherent/semi-coherent L1 2 nanoprecipitates released inter-lattice strain.

title: A single-molecular bridge for simultaneously passivating dual-interface defects to fabricate high-efficiency and stable perovskite solar cells
abstract: We introduced LAUA between SnO 2 and the perovskite film, which improves interface contact and energy level alignment. Experimental results show that PSCs exhibit a power conversion efficiency of 26.21% and excellent stability.

title: TMSI–TOP: a dual-function precursor for anion exchange and surface passivation via phosphonium iodide formation in CsPb(Br/I) 3 perovskite nanocrystals
abstract: A novel TMSI–TOP precursor enables stable red CsPb(Br/I) 3 PeNCs, where phosphonium iodide forms and TMSOH aids defect passivation, yielding LEDs with 21.69% EQE at 669 nm and verified reliability.

title: Ball milling modification of carbon nanomaterials for supercapacitors and rechargeable alkali-ion batteries
abstract: Ball milling is a solvent-free, scalable technique that enhances the structure and properties of carbon nanomaterials, significantly improving their performance in supercapacitors and alkali-ion batteries for advanced energy storage.

title: Porphyrins with di-alkoxy-thiophene side chains for organic solar cells
abstract: Porphyrins with optimized di-alkoxy-thiophene side chains enable high-efficiency binary and ternary OSCs; sidechain engineering and PC 61 BM incorporation synergistically enhance charge transport, suppress recombination and boost device stability.

title: Self-similar architectures for pressure-tolerant and mechanically durable superamphiphobic coatings
abstract: Pressure-tolerant and mechanically durable superamphiphobic coatings are prepared by constructing self-similar hierarchical micro-/nanostructures via a phase-separation-assisted spray-coating strategy.

title: Ferroelectric switchable valleytricity in 2D multiferroic semiconductors
abstract: Efficient control of the valley index is of great importance for both fundamental research and device applications, yet it remains a challenging problem.

title: Dual-site molecular passivation at the buried interface for high-efficiency and stable perovskite solar cells
abstract: Bifunctional DPN optimizes the buried interface of inverted PSCs via dual-site coordination. It passivates Pb 2+ defects and promotes vertical crystallization, yielding a champion PCE of 25.3% with enhanced stability.

title: High-entropy layered oxide electrocatalyst derived from spent battery cathodes for overall water splitting and 2,5 hydroxymethylfurfural (HMF) oxidation
abstract: Low-temperature upcycling of battery waste into a Ni-rich high-entropy layered oxide electrocatalyst for simultaneous H 2 and FDCA co-production.

title: Stoichiometry-driven structural superiority and crossover from intrinsic band-like to hopping transport in mixed cation perovskite single crystals
abstract: Stoichiometry-dependent charge transport in MA 1− x FA x PbI 3 single crystals is elucidated to reveal the structure-optoelectronic performance nexus. The optimal x = 0.52 composition yields a superior mobility of 52.1 cm 2 V −1 s −1 at 80 K.

title: A desert locust-inspired wavelength-selective polarization vision system using two-dimensional ferroelectric heterostructures
abstract: A bionic polarization vision system (BPVS) featuring polarization sensitivity and wavelength selectivity, in combination with a convolutional neural network (UBPVS-net), is designed to enable effective reconstruction of polarization images.

title: Noble metal-p-block-element intermetallics with d–p orbital hybridization for highly efficient electrocatalysis
abstract: A series of noble metal-p-block-element intermetallics with d–p orbital hybridization have been developed for efficient electrocatalysis.

title: A water-recyclable, robust, and self-healing sugar-based supramolecular network enabled by Maillard-analogous initialization of polymerization
abstract: A Maillard-inspired tough sugar-based supramolecular network enabling chemical recycling and self-healing, driven by water dissociation of the α-carbonyl imine bond.

title: From muscle to motion: GaIn nanoparticle-TPU core/shell mesh electrodes for intelligent prosthesis
abstract: GaIn NPs-TPU core/shell mesh electrode enables an EMG wristband that offers stretchability, and a high SNR. Combined with a machine learning algorithm, it interprets muscle activity to precise motion control of a robotic prosthetic hand device.

title: Hydrogel-based thermal management for photovoltaic safe operation and power generation enhancement
abstract: This review thoroughly analyzed the challenge and potential solutions of hydrogel-based PV cooling from lab research to practical applications.

title: A bioinspired polyoxo-titanium cluster for efficient photocatalytic CO 2 reduction assisted by hydrogen bonding
abstract: A bioinspired polyoxo-titanium cluster uses hydrogen-bonding traction for highly efficient photocatalytic CO 2 reduction.

title: Flexible thermoelectric materials and devices for sensing applications
abstract: Schematic of the thermoelectric conversion process and an outline of the various sensing applications of FTE materials and FTE devices.

title: Corn-flower-like transparent, anti-moth protein and self-cleaning coatings for vehicle windshields
abstract: A hyperbranched fluorosilicone design creates a corn-flower-like perfluoroalkyl array, enabling transparent, ultralow-energy, anti-fouling coatings for durable windshield protection.

title: Digital shape-morphing thermo-mechanical metamaterials
abstract: DSTM converts mechanical inputs through thermal processing into discrete digital outputs. that enables programmable logic operations and information storage through controlled mechanical deformation and temperature-driven state transitions.

title: Materials for thermochemical energy storage and conversion: attributes for low-temperature applications
abstract: The storage and management of heat via thermochemical reactions can enhance energy efficiency and expedite decarbonization. Here, materials performance criteria are described for low-temperature applications such as the heating/cooling of buildings.

title: Self-healing redox chemistry in Cu–TiO 2 photocatalysts for enhanced hydrogen production
abstract: Methanol photoreforming driven by a self-healing Cu redox loop that regenerates active Cu 2 O to prevent deactivation—enabling durable, scalable, and efficient solar hydrogen production.

title: Amorphous–crystalline transition-induced hollowing of covalent organic frameworks: a structural evolution boosting uranium adsorption
abstract: A phosphate-rich hollow covalent organic framework (PA-H-COF) was prepared by a template method through the amorphous-to-crystalline transformation. Its uranium adsorption performance is superior to that of traditional non-hollow materials.

title: Concurrent selective laser sintering and graphitization of polyimide microparticles into functional and flexible 3D structures for energy storage and sensing
abstract: The synergistic integration of selective laser sintering and laser induced graphitization with polyimide and its hybrid microparticles enables the precise fabrication of 2D/3D patterned and conductive graphene structures.

title: Synchronous repair of intrinsic thermal conductivity and mechanical performance of aramid nanofiber films through hydrogen-bonding reconstruction
abstract: A thermal-annealing-directed molecular engineering strategy is developed to reconfigure the chain conformation and hydrogen-bonding networks of ANF, achieving record-high intrinsic thermal conductivity and tensile strength.

title: Machine learning pipelines for the design of solid-state electrolytes
abstract: ML-driven closed-loop pipeline SSE discovery. The cycle integrates AI-based prediction with experiment and application to identify promising candidates, with experimental data continuously refining the models for accelerated materials discovery.

title: Correction: Ice-assisted cryogenic embrittlement grinding for surfactant-free fabrication of soft nanomaterials
abstract: Correction for ‘Ice-assisted cryogenic embrittlement grinding for surfactant-free fabrication of soft nanomaterials’ by Yangjie Fu et al. , Mater. Horiz. , 2026, https://doi.org/10.1039/D6MH00347H.

title: Interface storage of vanadium based materials in zinc-ion batteries
abstract: Vanadium based composite materials utilize “job-sharing” mechanism to resolve Ragone conflict.

title: A self-regulating wearable OLED patch for accelerated wound healing via photobiomodulation-triggered drug delivery
abstract: Flexible, wavelength-tunable OLED patches promote wound healing via PBM and leverage PBM-induced ROS as endogenous triggers for on-demand drug release, establishing an integrated phototherapy–drug delivery platform for combination therapy.

title: Durable superhydrophobic coatings enable scalable offshore photovoltaics for global energy decarbonization
abstract: A robust, transparent superhydrophobic coating with radiative cooling capabilities enhances the durability of marine photovoltaics, offering a scalable solution for global decarbonization.

title: Multimodal human-in-the-loop artificial intelligence with affective feedback for accelerated high-entropy alloy discovery
abstract: Human-centric material design strategy is developed through a multimodal human-in-the-loop artificial intelligence approach integrating affective feedback, for accelerated discovery of high-performance high entropy alloys.

title: LCST-phase-separated porous liquid metal-filled hydrogel actuators with fast electro-response, enhanced strength, and low electric field
abstract: A liquid metal-filled porous hydrogel actuator shows rapid, large-angle bending under low electric fields. LCST-induced porosity and LM microparticles enable efficient electro-responsiveness for soft robotics.

title: The carbon cost of materials discovery: Can machine learning really accelerate the discovery of new photovoltaics?
abstract: Simulations and machine learning have promised to be a powerful complement to experimental efforts to discover new photovoltaics – we assess the carbon cost of these approaches.

title: Bioinspired, compositionally graded cellulose-based dielectrics with Schottky-engineered interfaces for high-performance and sustainable energy storage
abstract: Inspired by black swan feathers, compositionally graded cellulose/P(VDF–HFP)-BCZT films were designed. The down-graded trilayer achieved superior energy storage ( W rec = 38.73 J cm −3 , η = 79.39%), rapid discharge, and excellent fire resistance.

title: Magnetothermal nanocomposite scaffolds with dual stimulation for synergistic drug delivery and cellular modulation in wound healing
abstract: Exploring the intricate mechanisms by which hyperthermia influences cellular and tissue behavior presents a significant challenge in tissue engineering.

title: Mechanically robust polyurethane elastomers enabled by soft-segment-regulated hydrogen bonds and microphase separation for ultrasound imaging medical catheters
abstract: Soft-segment engineering tailors microphase separation and strain-induced crystallization (SIC) in TPUs, achieving a strong–flexible balance. The material exhibits high toughness, antibacterial capabilities, and improved ultrasound imaging.

title: Dual-target, high-capacity removal of microplastics and dyes from water using a recyclable sponge monolith
abstract: Once adsorbed onto the CT/AC monolith, pollutants including microplastics and dyes, along with the entire material, are converted into graphene via flash Joule heating, effectively preventing secondary pollution.

title: Orbital-level engineering of bonding networks to modulate halogen migration in lead-free double perovskites
abstract: Ion migration is a continuous process of chemical bond reconfiguration, wherein antibonding hybridization and alignment serve as precise descriptors governing halide ion migration.

title: Recent progress of narrowband iridium( iii ) complexes for organic light-emitting diodes
abstract: In this review, recent advances in narrowband Ir( iii ) phosphors are summarized, elucidating their structure–property relationship and discussing the molecular design for organic light-emitting diodes with high color purity and excellent efficiency.

title: Cysteine-rich peptide for chiral self-assembly: a multi-functional antioxidant with novel SH-mediated ROS-responsive disassembly from natural medicine
abstract: Unveiling pangolin-derived peptides and a cysteine-rich motif for chiral self-assembly: discovering naturally assembled bioactive nanomaterials from traditional medicine.

title: Introducing plasmonic antenna nanojunctions into nanocavity structures to confine and enhance their local electromagnetic fields
abstract: Nanostructures with plasmonic nanoantennae and plasmonic nanocavities can confine and enhance local electromagnetic fields, with Raman enhancement factors 5 to 10 times higher than that of the sample without these two effects.

title: Recent advances in patterned bio-inspired cholesteric liquid crystals: fabrication, stimuli-responsive mechanisms, and smart optical applications
abstract: Patterned bio-inspired cholesteric liquid crystals are reviewed as programmable photonic materials, focusing on helical design, fabrication strategies, stimuli responsiveness, and multifunctional applications.

title: Pressure-driven interconversion between Ruddlesden–Popper and Dion–Jacobson phases in two-dimensional hybrid perovskites via supramolecular interactions
abstract: Stereospecific cations induce nDJ and nRP structures in 2D perovskites, enabling pressure-driven RP–DJ interconversion, photoluminescence enhancement, and reversible mechanochromism via supramolecular modulation.

title: Candidate electro-optic molecular crystals for future optoelectronic integration
abstract: We present novel electro-optic (EO) molecular crystals suitable for future optoelectronic integration that combine high EO activity, thin-film processability, and thermal stability.

title: Self-stratified photonic radiative cooling composites with asymmetric thermal conductivity
abstract: High-power outdoor electronics, such as 5G base stations, need energy-efficient thermal management.

title: Fundamentals and mechanistic insights for ammonium-ion energy storage: spotlight on metal–organic frameworks
abstract: The development of efficient and sustainable energy storage technologies necessitates a deep understanding of charge storage mechanisms and rational materials design.

title: Shape programming of liquid crystal elastomers by two-stage wavelength-selective photopolymerization
abstract: We report a method to produce two-way shape memory liquid crystal elastomers using the orthogonal photo-crosslinking of acrylate and oxetane groups.

title: Emergence of magnetic monopole-like behavior in iron oxide nanoparticles grafted with chiral brushes: a chiral induced spin selectivity manifestation
abstract: We examine the magnetic properties of ∼20 nm single domain and ∼200 nm multidomain iron oxide nanoparticles that are surface functionalized with poly( l - or d -phenylalanine) chiral brushes of variable length.

title: Thermoreversible adhesives with precisely temperature-controlled detachment enabled by temperature-responsive crystalline domains
abstract: Thermoreversible adhesives allow for on-demand bonding and debonding on diverse surfaces through thermal stimulation.

title: Magnetochiral anisotropy induced nonreciprocal transport in Si/SiO x /ZnO heterostructures: a path to advanced rectification and spintronics
abstract: A quasi-two-dimensional electron gas in Si/SiO x /ZnO exhibits nonreciprocal charge transport in all planes via spin–orbit coupling and skew scattering, enabling promising applications in directional nonlinear electronics and efficient rectifiers.

title: Inverse design of thermally active composite via policy-transferred reinforcement learning
abstract: Policy-transferred reinforcement learning enables sample-efficient inverse design of thermally active composites with programmable 4D shape transformation.

title: Uncompromised reinforcement: polysiloxane with 100% room-temperature self-healing and antifouling
abstract: Polysiloxane composites integrate uncompromised reinforcement, 100% self-healing and antifouling.

title: Predicting how fast crystals grow at the free surface of molecular glasses
abstract: Crystal growth at the surface of molecular glasses is much faster than in bulk. Here, we show that collective small displacements provide a predictive framework able to captures growth rates across diverse organic molecules.

title: Reconciling Sensitivity and Breathability in Flexible Iontronic Pressure Sensors via Hierarchical Fibrous Architecture
abstract: Breathability is an essential property for wearable pressure sensors used in long-term physiological monitoring. However, achieving high sensitivity while maintaining good breathability remains a significant challenge. Here, we report an...

title: A strippable catechol-terminated polyurethane coating for large-area radioactive cesium decontamination
abstract: A strippable catechol-terminated polyurethane (CPU) coating was developed as a fast, highly adhesive, and effective method for radioactive Cs + decontamination.

title: Hierarchical strengthening and toughening design of superalloy joints via a chemically complex intermetallic alloy-modified diffusion bonding strategy
abstract: The design and fabrication of advanced interlayer materials are pivotal for the diffusion bonding (DB) of precision and slip-structure turbine components in aero-engines.

title: Controllable and tunable conductive polymer/MXene hybrid composites for biosensor applications: recent advances, properties, and future directions
abstract: This review introduces the hybrid composites of conductive polymers and MXene, with a focus on their applications in non-invasive biosensors and wearable biomedical devices.

title: Pb–S coordination-assisted in situ ligand engineering of CsPbBr 3 nanocrystals using (3-mercaptopropyl)methyldimethoxysilane for enhanced robustness and optoelectronic properties
abstract: MPMDMS passivation enables perovskite NCs with superior stability, high luminescence efficiency, improved crystallinity, enhanced electron mobility, and reduced traps.

title: Stabilizing Pt–Fe dual-metal single atoms in ZIFs: a pathway to form heterogeneous catalysts
abstract: Dual-atom catalysts (DACs) show promise for enhanced catalytic performance through synergistic metal interactions, yet their formation and stability during high-temperature pyrolysis remain poorly understood.

title: Gigapascal yield strength at 900 °C in a boron-free TiAl complex alloy having excellent room temperature formability
abstract: A newly designed boron-free, Ti 50 Al 45 Nb 2.5 Mo 1.25 Ta 0.5 W 0.5 V 0.25 complex alloy, ( ρ = 4.12 ± 0.015 g cm −3 ), exhibited a compressive yield strength (YS) of ∼ 1.080 ± 0.029 GPa at 900 °C, in the as-cast state due to unusual β 0 phase dissolution.

title: Scalable and sustainable manufacturing of intermetallic nanocrystals for economical water splitting
abstract: Schematic of the roll-flow synthesis of Ni 3 Fe-ordered structure nanocrystals from a homogeneous precursor via controlled ultrafast radiative heating to achieve large-scale production.

title: Water- and oxygen-tolerant phosphorescent carbon nitrides enable visual hydrogel biosensing
abstract: All requirements for H 2 O/O 2 -tolerant room-temperature phosphorescence (RTP) are met by covalently modified carbon nitride. The exceptionally stable RTP in extreme pH conditions and nano-fragmentation enables afterglow hydrogel for Fe 3+ sensing.

title: Nanofibrillar conductive hydrogel adhesive for soft bioelectronic interfaces
abstract: A conductive nanofibrillar double-network hydrogel adhesive enables real-time, long-term monitoring of human motion and physiological signals, including electrocardiography (ECG), electromyography (EMG), and lung respiration.

title: Power-tunable multicolor upconversion in nanocrystals under single-wavelength excitation
abstract: In this work, we demonstrate for the first time tunable upconversion luminescence in three primary colors using a single excitation wavelength of 980 nm, via altering the excitation intensity.

title: High-performance transparent metal mesh electrodes utilizing a metal-vapor-desorption layer for organic light-emitting diode applications
abstract: High-resolution Ag mesh was directly evaporated on sensitive OLED stacks using a transfer-printed metal-vapor-desorption layer. The mesh electrodes showed a figure of merit above 10 4 , and transparent OLEDs with mesh top electrodes were demonstrated.

title: Recent advancements in heterostructure-based electrocatalysts for sustainable hydrogen production through seawater splitting
abstract: The growing demand for sustainable energy has positioned hydrogen (H 2 ) gas as a promising clean energy carrier.

title: Nano-architected GaN spinodoid metamaterials with tailorable anisotropic piezoelectric properties
abstract: Spinodoid topology engineering enables GaN metamaterials with enhanced piezoelectricity and tailored anisotropy, exhibiting emergent non-zero piezoelectric constants beyond crystallographic symmetry constraints.

title: Slippery when charged: hydration lubrication in hydrogels
abstract: Despite electrostatic attraction, hydration forces govern interfacial behavior in positively charged PVA–chitosan double-network hydrogels, establishing structure–property relationships that enable superlubricity.

title: Directional manipulation of bubble behavior on wettability gradient surfaces: mechanisms, strategies, and applications
abstract: This review systematically explores the directional manipulation of bubble behavior on wettability gradient surfaces: the mechanisms, strategies, and applications.

title: Decoupling shape retention from polymerization kinetics enables ambient-temperature 3D printing of polystyrene
abstract: A dual-curing 3D printing strategy enables the unprecedented direct ink writing of crosslinked polystyrene, breaking down the barriers of polymer additive manufacturing to thermosetting polymers.

title: Boosting wide-temperature solid-state lithium metal batteries by polyether–carbonate hybridization
abstract: By hybridizing polyether and polycarbonate electrolytes, the strategy of electronic cloud modulation addresses the issues of lithium–metal batteries with liquid electrolytes and propels the development of batteries for a wide range of temperatures.

title: Bone-targeted calcification-photothermal nanoplatform for synergistic tumor ablation and bone regeneration in breast cancer bone metastasis
abstract: A bone-targeted nanoplatform harnesses calcification-photothermal synergy for simultaneous tumor eradication and bone regeneration, establishing a new therapeutic paradigm for breast cancer bone metastasis.

title: Synthesis and pyroelectric response of disperse red 1 functionalized silicones: cyclic monomer, homopolymer, and block copolymer derivatives
abstract: Self-assembled H-aggregates in Disperse Red-functionalized polysiloxane yield spontaneous pyroelectricity. Remarkably, this material achieves pyroelectric performance comparable to that of PVDF processed under similar conditions.

title: A meniscus-inspired, environmentally robust, tough soybean-based adhesive
abstract: A meniscus-inspired tough and high-strength soybean meal adhesive with excellent coating performance, mildew resistance, and flame retardancy.

title: Machine learning (ML)-assisted development of 2D green catalysts to support sustainability
abstract: Combining green-synthesized 2D nanomaterials with ML for catalytic applications to support sustainability.

title: Self-adaptive ZrN coating enables stable acidic oxygen evolution on Co 3 O 4 through dynamic surface reconstruction
abstract: Adaptive ZrN coating reconstructs to conductive Zr 2 ON 2 /corrosive ZrO 2 dual-phase on Co 3 O 4 , enabling stable acidic OER (362 mV@10 mA cm −2 , >140 h) via suppressed Co over-oxidation.

title: Ultra-thick three-dimensional interpenetrating graphene electrode architectures for high volumetric density energy storage
abstract: A novel data-driven electrode structure design and additive manufacturing ensure superior material utilization with minimal ohmic losses and substantially enhance volumetric energy and power densities compared to conventional configurations.

title: Nano energy amplifier – the proposal and demonstration of triplet–triplet annihilation upconversion heterojunctions for photocatalysis using low-energy photons
abstract: Schematic of the TTA-UC heterojunction. MoS 2 sensitizes NIR photons and transfers energy to ZIF-FL emitters, converting low-energy light into visible photons that trigger photocatalytic degradation.

title: Skin-inspired high-strength, adhesive, healable and smart thermoregulation hydrogel sensors for multi-sensing via one-pot PET-RAFT
abstract: Smart hydrogel sensors with excellent physicochemical properties and exceptional sensitivity to multiple external stimuli demonstrate considerable potential in various simulations of human skin's functions. (Image created with BioGDP.com).

title: Unlocking the role of indium ions in stabilizing zinc negative electrodes in highly acidic electrolytes for high-voltage aqueous Zn batteries
abstract: Indium-containing surfaces are unfavorable for proton adsorption due to the weak hydrogen binding nature of indium. These surfaces can form on Zn through two pathways: replacement deposition during rest and dynamic alloying during electroplating.

title: Hierarchical morphology and interfacial dynamics in silane-free rubber nanocomposites: a SAXS-guided approach toward sustainable high-performance tire materials
abstract: This study presents a SAXS-guided tread model resolving occluded rubber and filler hierarchies, showing PEG promotes filler aggregation and interfacial slippage, yielding better WG, lower RR, and higher stiffness than TESPT in silane-free tires.

title: Suspension polymerization of bioelectronic interfaces on living cells
abstract: This work introduces an easy method to coat living cells with conductive polymers using enzyme-mediated polymerization. The resulting coatings are conductive and cytocompatible, offering a minimally invasive way to build bioelectronic interfaces.

title: DNA-based Bi-layered effervescent ejecting microneedle (BEE MN) for glucose-responsive insulin delivery
abstract: Microneedle-based drug delivery systems have gained increasing attention as minimally invasive alternatives to conventional injections, particularly for chronic diseases like diabetes.

title: Integrating intrinsic charge-trapping sites in an insulated MOF nanoparticle-based dielectric layer for effective photo/synaptic transistors
abstract: Based on metal–organic frameworks with diverse energy level structures, charge/energy transfer pathways between semiconductors and insulators are established, integrating effective photo/synaptic transistors to emulate brain/machine operation.

title: In situ synthesis of dispersed boride-induced high-performance brazed DD5 superalloy via the formation of a novel sandwich-structured interlayer
abstract: A novel sandwich-structured interlayer enhanced the shear strength of the brazed superalloy by 74.4% through converting trapped boron atoms into dispersed borides, thereby suppressing brittle phases.

title: Applying spoof-plasmonic metasurfaces to microwave sample preparation of biological samples
abstract: A 96-well microplate with a spoof-plasmonic metasurface on its underside and the resulting effects upon irradiation at 2.45 GHz.

title: Physically programmed vegan leather emulating the mechanical and sensory characteristics of animal leather from once-discarded gluten
abstract: Biodegradable, additive-free gluten leather mimicking animal leather's mechanics and tactility via depth-specific thermal and UV programming.

title: Surface group-governed charge storage in carbon dots for a visual memristor
abstract: Electric-field-driven regulation of surface functional groups in carbon dots modulates both the emission spectrum and the resistive state, achieving an integrated visual memristor.

title: Defect chemistry of mixed ionic–electronic conductors under light: halide perovskites as a master example
abstract: Shining light on a mixed conductor can induce shifts in stoichiometry and nonequilibrium in the ionic defect disorder, with implications for the optimization of devices like perovskite solar cells and the design of novel optoionic functionalities.

title: A dual-bioinspired structural design enables highly efficient microwave absorption and thermal insulation in SiC hybrid aerogels
abstract: This study proposes a dual bionic structure design SiC aerogel with hollow microtube and nanowire arrays. The wave-trapping architecture and multi-scale interfaces enable it to exhibit superior EMW absorption and thermal insulation performance.

title: Electron-beam-induced structural degradation in two representative 2D MOFs: a ligand-dependent comparison
abstract: Two-dimensional metal-organic frameworks (2D-MOFs) are promising for separations, sensing, and nano-optoelectronics. However, their integration with electron beam fabrication and imaging is limited by beam-induced structural damage. Here, we show that...

title: Synergistic integration of hydrogels and cold plasma for biomedical applications and therapeutics
abstract: The synergistic integration of hydrogels (HGs) and cold atmospheric plasma (CAP) represents a transformative advancement in biomaterials and plasma medicine, opening new pathways for next-generation therapeutics.

title: Advances in defect-engineered metal–support interactions for acidic oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: This review summarizes the progress in defect-engineered metal–support interactions (DMSIs) towards the acidic OER. It systematically discusses the synthetic strategies and DMSI–OER relationship, and highlights advanced characterization techniques.

title: Regulation of the grain boundary CeFe 2 phase via Cu addition for anisotropic Ce–Fe–B magnet fabrication
abstract: Cu doping was employed to reduce the melting point of the CeFe 2 phase, enabling the successful fabrication of anisotropic Ce–Fe–B magnets with a textured lath-like grain structure.

title: Chemical diversity encoded in symmetry: universal distortion rules and design principles for perovskites
abstract: The universal language of symmetry: order parameters link chemical composition to structure and physical properties in perovskites.

title: Giant thermal Hall effect in Topological Magnon Insulator Cr 3 Se 4 monolayer
abstract: Thermal Hall effect, the thermal analogue of Hall effect, has emerged as a key probe of charge-neutral excitations in topological magnons. Therefore, achieving the giant thermal Hall effect in topological...

title: Bioinspired design of hierarchical structured hydrogels with extraordinary lubrication and load-bearing capacity
abstract: Conventional homogeneous hydrogels typically face an inherent trade-off between mechanical strength and lubrication performance. Achieving high load-bearing capacity requires dense crosslinking and tight chain entanglement, which inevitably restrict water uptake...

title: Synergistic dual doping and directed structural engineering of 2D cobalt tellurium nanoarchitectures for practical-level overall water splitting
abstract: A dual-atom (Ru,Mn)-doped CoTe 2 catalyst with a Mo-directed porous nanosheet architecture exhibits small overpotentials for HER and OER, suggesting its high potential for green hydrogen generation.

title: In situ whisker reinforced multiphase bioceramic scaffolds with fluorine-activated osteogenesis
abstract: One-step solid-state reaction forms in situ whiskers in HAp ceramics, increasing compressive strength by ∼200%. F − -incorporated multiphase ceramics further improve mechanics and osteogenesis by activating mitochondria and boosting ATP production.

title: A tellurium-free GeSbSe thin film for reliable selector-only memory operation
abstract: By eliminating Te-driven bonding networks, a Te-free GeSbSe selector-only memory achieves a stable, wide memory margin under 50 ns pulses, with low write current and fast switching, providing a clear materials design rule for reliable CXL devices.

title: Thermal-driven H-bond reconfiguration for bioinspired high-strength anisotropic supramolecular hydrogels
abstract: Inspired by organisms, we develop a hydrogen bond reconfiguration strategy to build supramolecular hydrogels via thermally reversible alignment. Quenching pre-stretched networks yields anisotropic hydrogels with outstanding mechanical performance.

title: Multifunctional PVA/MXQDs hydrogels for integrated flexible strain sensing and solid-state energy storage systems
abstract: The development of multifunctional conductive hydrogels that integrate high electrochemical performance, mechanical robustness, and sensing capability is essential for next-generation wearable electronics.

title: Strategic molecular conformation engineering enables significantly enhanced high-temperature energy storage in polyetherimide
abstract: To accommodate the advancement of next-generation power electronics toward high-level integration and miniaturization, it is imperative to develop polymer dielectrics that maintain superior energy storage performance under thermal extremes.

title: Zigzag-type metastructures of metallic glass with precisely tunable emissivity for thermal infrared camouflage technology
abstract: Zigzag-type metallic glass films fabricated by glancing angle deposition exhibit anisotropic optical properties with precisely tunable emissivity, enabling dynamic control of thermal radiation and effective infrared camouflage.

title: Oxygen-intercalated Ruddlesden–Popper nickelate: giant resistive switching and emergent multi-electronic phase control
abstract: Reversible resistivity modulation of seven orders of magnitude at 250 K was achieved using (NdNiO 3 ) n :NdO heterojunctions.

title: Defect-engineered ferroelectricity and magnetoelectric coupling in LaFeO 3 thin films
abstract: Antisite-defect engineering turns epitaxial LaFeO 3 into a room-temperature multiferroic: a polar R3c phase yields stable magnetism and switchable ferroelectricity, providing a general design route for multiferroic orthoferrites.

title: Post-functionalization modification as a modular strategy for size-selective fluorescence response of single-walled carbon nanotubes to polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons
abstract: Single-walled carbon nanotubes (SWCNTs) with tailored functionalization serve as optically responsive nanoparticles, but when encapsulated by chirality-selective polymers, they often remain inert to analytes.

title: Covalently sewing dual-sided Janus coatings via photoiniferter surface chain extension
abstract: Visible-light photoiniferter RAFT enables covalent “sewing” of weak Janus bilayer boundaries into robust interfaces. The resulting polyPFPA/polyOFPA dual-sided coating combines high adhesion and hydrophobic shielding for durable corrosion resistance.

title: Template-guided self-assembly of semiconducting polymers in crystallizable solvent-induced nanoconfinement
abstract: The morphology and chain orientation of conjugated polymer films strongly influence their charge transport properties.

title: Ion-channel-mediated gradient acceptor distribution for hard lead-free piezoceramics
abstract: Introducing a second-phase KT to create a donor ion gradient successfully overcomes the inherent d 33 - Q m trade-off. This strategy enables both high piezoelectric constant and mechanical quality factor in high-performance lead-free ceramics.

title: Redefining molecular design and exciton dynamics in single-component organic photocatalysts for efficient solar-to-hydrogen conversion
abstract: A major challenge in organic single-component photocatalysts (SCPCs) for hydrogen (H 2 ) generation is their intrinsically inefficient exciton separation and charge generation.

title: Ice-assisted cryogenic embrittlement grinding for surfactant-free fabrication of soft nanomaterials
abstract: We introduce ICE-grinding, overcoming chemistry constraints and contamination in soft nanoparticle synthesis. Cryogenic vitrification embrittles gels via glassy ice, directing nanoscale fracture while preserving polymer networks for nanomedicine.

title: Engineered covalent organic framework nanomotor for amplified cuproptosis therapy via Wnt/β-catenin signaling inhibition
abstract: Engineered covalent organic framework (COF) nanomotors for the first nanomotor capable of simultaneously delivering copper ions and the Wnt/β-catenin inhibitor LF3.

title: Harnessing pseudoelasticity in SMA-based negative stiffness mechanical metamaterials for superior strength and recoverability
abstract: A pseudoelasticity-enabled design strategy is proposed for negative stiffness curved beam metamaterials using shape memory alloys (SMAs), overcoming the traditional trade-off between high strength and recoverable energy dissipation.

title: Reprocessable crosslinked polymer dielectrics for high-temperature capacitive energy storage
abstract: Polymer dielectrics for mainstream capacitive energy-storage technologies are expected to operate reliably under extreme thermal conditions, yet remain persistently limited by the long-standing trade-off between electrical insulation and thermal tolerance....

title: Introduction to the themed collection on nanocatalysis
abstract: Huabin Zhang, Jennifer Strunk, Marcella Lusardi, Tianyi Ma, Vivek Polshettiwar, and Wee-Jun Ong introduce the Materials Horizons and Nanoscale Horizons cross-journal themed collection on Nanocatalysis.

title: Quasi-heavy atom effect for room-temperature phosphorescence
abstract: This study introduces the quasi-heavy atom effect, showing that polyvinyl alcohol’s abundant hydroxyl groups mimic the heavy atom effect, enabling room-temperature phosphorescence from molecules doped within the PVA matrix.

title: Correction: A silk fibroin-based hydrogel desensitizer achieving 660 µm dentin tubule occlusion for dentin hypersensitivity treatment
abstract: Correction for ‘A silk fibroin-based hydrogel desensitizer achieving 660 µm dentin tubule occlusion for dentin hypersensitivity treatment’ by Kuangdi Xin et al. , Mater. Horiz. , 2025, 12 , 4177–4192, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4MH01927J.

title: Next-generation borophene nanostructures for dual-domain sensing: from structural optimisation to biomedical and environmental applications
abstract: This review focuses on the synthesis, properties, and sensing mechanisms of borophene-derived nanostructures (nanosheets, quantum dots, and nanocomposites), with particular emphasis on their applications in environmental and biomedical detection.

title: Supramolecular net-suppressor drives tumor vascular-immune microenvironment remodeling with spatiotemporal synchronization for renal cancer therapy
abstract: Our RING strategy employs a cyclic peptide integrating three modules: (i) dual VEGF/Tie2 targeting for vascular remodeling; (ii) a β-sheet self-assembly motif forming nanofibers; (iii) a D-TIGIT blocking peptide to restore anti-tumor immunity.

title: A low interfacial-toughness self-segregating thermoset for large-scale ice-shedding coating application
abstract: Inserting soft PDMS domains into a hard PU matrix leads to a low interfacial toughness material with high mechanical stability. Properties are impacted by the coating's thickness and type of PDMS but not by the size of domains/amount of PDMS.

title: Correction: Harnessing weak monomer miscibility to create porous microdomains in polymer electrolytes for zinc-ion batteries
abstract: Correction for ‘Harnessing weak monomer miscibility to create porous microdomains in polymer electrolytes for zinc-ion batteries’ by Lei Zhang et al. , Mater. Horiz. , 2026, https://doi.org/10.1039/d6mh00453a.

title: Dramatic enhancement of nonlinear optical signals in distinct two-dimensional materials
abstract: We developed an air-gap-suspended nanocavity that enhances SHG and Raman signals in 2D materials, enabling precise characterization and offering a platform to study their intrinsic properties.

title: Efficient separation of the NCM cathode material and Al foil from spent lithium-ion batteries with oxalic acid under mild conditions
abstract: Green and low-cost oxalic acid can be used as a selective agent to separate the NCM cathode layer and Al foil of SLIBs under mild conditions, providing a practical, efficient and eco-friendly recycling method for the NCM cathode materials.

title: In situ construction of all-solid-state Z-scheme g-C3N4/ZnNCN/ZnO with carbodiimide bridges for superior photocatalytic H2O2 production
abstract: Photocatalytic production of Hydrogen peroxide (H2O2) via g-C3N4 represents an ideal solar-to-chemical conversion process, yet its efficiency is severely constrained by sluggish charge separation kinetics. Here, this bottleneck is overcome...

title: High-performance dynamic random access memory capacitor with an equivalent oxide thickness of 0.31 nm via stepwise cycling in Y-doped Hf 0.5 Zr 0.5 O 2 thin films
abstract: This study investigates the field-induced ferroelectric (FFE) characteristics and dynamic random access memory (DRAM) capacitor performance of Y-doped Hf 0.5 Zr 0.5 O 2 (Y:Hf 0.5 Zr 0.5 O 2 ) thin films grown by atomic layer deposition (ALD).

title: Inflammatory cytokine scavenging and macrophage metabolism reprogramming to orchestrate inflammatory bone regeneration
abstract: M2 macrophage membrane-camouflaged selenium nanospheres’ dual-action cytokine decoy and metabolic reprogramming orchestrate inflammatory bone regeneration.

title: Ultra-high-power-efficiency organic light-emitting diodes based on a hot-exciton-assisted exciplex (HEAE) system
abstract: A novel strategy using hot-exciton materials to recover exciton energy is presented, leading to record-breaking power efficiencies in OLEDs—an advancement with strong relevance for next-generation display and lighting applications.

title: A “drug-carrier homologation” cardiac patch for myocardial infarction therapy via month-long controlled H 2 S release
abstract: A disulfide/trisulfide cross-linked polymeric cardiac patch enables a month-long and adequate H 2 S release profile for comprehensive myocardial infarction therapy.

title: A microencapsulated linseed oil adhesive based on photosensitive indication: a smart material for visualized curing monitoring
abstract: A biomass-derived core-shell adhesive with a pressure-responsive architecture is proposed to enable controllable adhesion and on-demand release. Integrated photochromic spiropyran serves as a visual curing indicator, allowing real-time monitoring.

title: Design principles of adhesive hydrogels for biomedical application
abstract: Current adhesive hydrogel designs lack multifunctional integration. We propose four key principles derived from holistic tissue pathology analysis to guide next-generation hydrogels and their clinical translation.

title: Molecular tailoring of electrolyte solvents for high-performance lithium–metal batteries beyond temperature and voltage boundaries
abstract: Electrolyte optimization is recognized as a critical strategy for enhancing both the long-term cycling stability and safety performance of lithium-ion batteries.

title: A battery-free, wireless graphene pressure sensor for machine learning-assisted posture classification and VR/AR visualization in smart healthcare environments
abstract: A wireless graphene-based sensing platform for battery-free pressure monitoring is developed. Leveraging machine learning and VR/AR interfaces, the system provides intuitive, real-time posture analysis for advanced smart healthcare environments.
